title: Research progress and perspective of transparent electrode materials in flexible perovskite solar cells
abstract: This article reviews the application progress of transparent electrode materials in F-PSCs, detailing their advantages, disadvantages, and commercialization prospects, aims to explore the potential application of F-PSCs in wearable devices and B-IPV.

title: Correction: Unlocking the loading limit in single-atom photocatalysts via defect-induced metal trapping
abstract: Correction for ‘Unlocking the loading limit in single-atom photocatalysts via defect-induced metal trapping’ by Laihao Liu et al. , Nanoscale , 2026, 18 , 4249–4257, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5nr04888e.

title: Circularity-engineered functional 2D materials: advances and commercialization insights for photocatalytic degradation of persistent contaminants
abstract: This review comprehensively presents circularity engineered 2D material photocatalysts for degrading persistent contaminants, covering material advances, green synthesis from waste sources, scalability challenges, and commercialization pathways.

title: Engineered S-scheme g-C 3 N 4 /MnO 2 heterostructures for integrated photo-rechargeable supercapacitors with enhanced energy storage performance
abstract: Engineered S-scheme g-C 3 N 4 /MnO 2 heterostructures for next-generation photo-rechargeable supercapacitors with enhanced energy storage.

title: Site-controlled Ge hut wire-based multiple quantum dots with integrated charge sensing capability
abstract: Site-controlled Ge hut wires enable fully tunable multiple quantum dots with mutual charge sensing. Parallel nanowire architectures allow transitions from single to triple quantum dots and reveal edge-state effects.

title: Cation-exchange and lateral ripening in wurtzite-like CuInSe 2 formation from weissite-like Cu 2− x Se
abstract: Weissite-like Cu 2− x Se nanoplates convert to wurtzite-like CuInSe 2 via In 3+ cation exchange while preserving the hexagonal Se 2− framework, revealing a coupled pathway of cation exchange and anisotropic lateral ripening.

title: Zwitterionic cross-linked lipoic acid nanocarriers for oral drug delivery
abstract: Sulfobetaine-modified cLAS nanoparticles reduce surface charge (−68 mV to −11 mV), which minimizes electrostatic repulsion with intestinal barriers, leverages the poly(disulfide) backbone's penetration ability, and enhances oral delivery.

title: Nanoscale carbonaceous materials via element hyperaccumulation for electrochemical desalination application
abstract: A new and green biosynthesis path of a nanoscale carbon for electrochemical desalination application.

title: High anisotropic carrier mobility and strong optical absorption in a biphenylene-like BC 3 monolayer
abstract: This work reports a new anisotropic biphenylene-like BC 3 monolayer with high anisotropic carrier mobility, showing promise for applications in field-effect transistors.

title: Sacrificial MoO 4 2− containing Ni/Co-(pre)catalysts: understanding of active structure and Fe-dynamics under steady-state conditions in alkaline oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: For long-term alkaline water oxidation with Ni- and Co-based catalysts, a solution-phase excess of Fe is required to stabilize catalytic performance, albeit in small quantities.

title: Sustainable hydrogen peroxide oxidation of carboxylated cellulose nanocrystals: efficient modulation of carboxyl content, hydrophilicity, and particle size for tablet formulation and drug release
abstract: H 2 O 2 -oxidized cellulose nanocrystals exhibit high surface area, tunable particle size, and excellent tablet hardness, disintegration, and drug release.

title: Nanoscale topology of γH2AX and 53BP1 foci in U87 cancer cells and normal NHDF after high-LET radiation-induced DSB repair
abstract: When Nanocosmos Meets the Macrocosmos: Heavy-Ion Cosmic Radiation Damages Astronaut DNA—Nuclear Chromatin Under Accelerated Heavy Ion Assault and DNA Repair.

title: Multifunctional nonlinear photonic devices based on spatial self-phase modulation in InP nanosheets
abstract: We report the third-order nonlinear susceptibility of single-layer InP nanosheets. By taking advantage of the superior electronic coherence and optical stability of InP nanosheets, we design and demonstrate four types of nonlinear photonic devices.

title: Two-photon photoluminescence excitation spectra of perovskite nanocrystals in a glass matrix
abstract: Two-photon excitation reveals hidden optical transitions in glass-embedded perovskite nanocrystals and demonstrates an enhanced nonlinear optical response.

title: Synergistically modified lithium-rich manganese-based cathodes via Zn doping and Al 2 O 3 coating for enhanced lithium-ion batteries
abstract: LNCM@Zn2@Al 2 O 3 was prepared via a one-step wet chemical method. Zn-doping modifies LNCM's lattice, Al 2 O 3 strengthens interfaces, and their synergy boosts performance, with its Li + diffusion coefficient tenfold higher than that of the uncoated sample.

title: Iron-induced phase engineering for high color-purity blue LEDs in perovskites
abstract: Iron additive-induced phase engineering in quasi-2D perovskites enables reduced emission FWHM and enhanced blue emission efficiency in PeLEDs.

title: Molecularly modified ultrathin Al 2 O 3 layers as proton-conductive, oxygen-impermeable nanomembranes for catalytic surfaces
abstract: Ultrathin inorganic oxide coatings can improve selectivity in photo- and electrocatalysis, but they also bury active sites and impede transport of the desired reactants.

title: Electronic structure modulation in Ag nanoparticle-Ag 2 Cu 2 O 3 catalyst: a strategy for stable and enhanced oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Ag@Ag 2 Cu 2 O 3 catalyst achieves excellent oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) activity and durability by leveraging an interfacial synergy that optimizes oxygen binding and facilitates ORR.

title: Kinetically controlled self-assembly of atom-precise gold nanoclusters into multicolor emissive supramolecular assemblies
abstract: A series of multicolor emissive nanofibers were obtained by self-assembly from atomic-precise Au nanoclusters. The kinetically controlled structural growth and the assembly-induced emission enhancement mechanisms were unraveled.

title: Predictive modeling of optical and electrical coupling in silver nanowire networks for stretchable transparent electrodes
abstract: We present a computational model and simulation to investigate the relationship between optical transmittance and the relative sheet resistance change as a function of the area fraction in stretchable conductive AgNW networks.

title: High-temperature molecular beam epitaxy of hexagonal boron nitride monolayers on carbon nanotubes
abstract: Epitaxial layers of hBN were grown on CNTs to form coaxial hBN/CNT heterostructures, which was confirmed by STEM-EELS. Photoluminescence revealed the first evidence of intrinsic exciton recombination in quasi-1D hBN and a 5.95 eV indirect bandgap.

title: Minimal water content tailoring of ZrO 2 nanocrystals via nonaqueous sol–gel synthesis
abstract: Zirconium dioxide (ZrO 2 ) nanocrystals were synthesized via a nonaqueous sol–gel (NASG) route using zirconium butoxide in benzyl alcohol under solvothermal conditions and high metal alkoxide concentrations, and controlled ppm-level water contents.

title: Rational design of benzoimidazoisoindolone-linked conjugated ladder polymer for enhanced visible-light-driven photocatalytic water oxidation
abstract: A benzoimidazoisoindolone-linked coplanar ladder polymer was rationally designed and exhibited a 2-times higher visible-light-driven photocatalytic water oxidation performance than its imide-linked linear analogue.

title: Synergistic carbon black-supported nickel–iron hydroxide nanocomposite for selective electrochemical discrimination of dihydroxybenzene isomers
abstract: Present work demonstrates the electrochemical detection and discrimination of dihydroxybenzene isomers i.e. catechol and hydroquinone over carbon black-supported nickel–iron hydroxide nanocomposite.

title: Two-dimensional conducting polymers: synthetic strategies and emerging applications in sensor technologies
abstract: This review summarizes synthetic strategies for 2D conducting polymers and clarifies how controlled thickness, crystallinity, and porosity translate into ultrathin, low-noise, flexible sensors for gas, pressure, and biosensing applications.

title: High and rapid perfluorooctanoic acid capture by MOF-818 via synergistic adsorption
abstract: The persistent environmental contamination and toxicity of perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) necessitate adsorbents that combine high capacity, fast kinetics, and robust recyclability. Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) offer tunable porosity and functionality, yet...

title: Synergistic effects of metallic and non-metallic element-doped electrocatalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction: a review
abstract: Heteroatom doping with metallic and non-metallic elements changes the electronic structure, increasing active site density, conductivity and surface area for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: Advances in visible and ultraviolet self-powered photodetectors: exploring 2D materials and Janus heterostructures
abstract: Recent advancements in 2D materials, including graphene, TMDCs, Janus structures, MXenes, and perovskite-based devices, have enabled the development of diverse self-powered photodetectors that offer broad spectral responses and flexible designs. Images reproduced with permission; full details in pap…

title: Rapid assembly of fluorescence enhanced plasmonic dimers for intracellular single-particle imaging
abstract: A scheme of fluorescence enhanced plasmonic dimers.

title: Flexible and efficient triboelectric nanogenerators based on PVDF and boron nitride composite yarns and mats
abstract: . BN-incorporated PVDF yarn TENGs deliver ~113% higher power density than pristine PVDF and outperform mats, offering a scalable route toward self-powered smart textiles and wearable energy harvesting.

title: Single-atom Cu 1 /ZrO 2 coupled with SAPO-34 enhances CO 2 hydrogenation to light olefins
abstract: The bifunctional catalyst composed of ZrO 2 -supported Cu single atoms and SAPO-34 breaks the activity-selectivity trade-off in CO 2 hydrogenation to light olefins, enabling high-value conversion of CO 2 .

title: Substrate-driven modulation of interfacial charge transfer dynamics in cobalt phthalocyanine–2D material van der Waals heterostructures
abstract: The CHC approach reveals substrate-dependent electron transfer dynamics in CoPc–2D heterostructures, highlighting how 2D materials modulate interfacial charge transfer.

title: Control of the photoluminescence bandwidth of ZnTeSe quantum dots by suppressing the Te cluster effect
abstract: To examine effect of Te spatial confinement on Te clustering, ZnTeSe QDs were prepared with center or surface-doped Te. Center-doped ZnTeSe QDs effectively suppressed Te clustering, showing PL at 531 nm with a narrow FWHM of 39 nm and a high QY of 89%.

title: Chitosan conjugated cyclodextrin nanocomposite loaded with antibiotic-adjuvant combinations remediates multi-drug resistant Staphylococcus aureus infection in CD-1 mice model of bovine mastitis
abstract: Bovine mastitis (BM), resulting from intramammary infection, is one of the costliest diseases in animal agriculture. Nano-enabled Antibacterial Combination Therapy (NeACT) holds promise for BM treatment.

title: Rational design of self-assembled monolayer composition for efficient perovskite/Si tandem solar cells
abstract: The co-adsorption of MeO-4PACz and Br-4PACz enhances the energy-level alignment and hole selectivity at the perovskite/SAM interface, yielding higher V OC in single-junction devices and 28.02% efficiency in 2T perovskite/Si tandem solar cells.

title: Fine-tuning and electronic modulation of AuPdCu nanoflowers assembled with nanowires for robust ethanol oxidation reaction performance
abstract: The nanoflower-like AuPdCu assembled by nanowires is designed for robust ethanol oxidation reactions.

title: Synergistic optimization of thermoelectric performance in Ag 2 Se through MoSe 2 decoration
abstract: Interface engineering can effectively reduce thermal conductivity in thermoelectric materials, resulting in enhanced thermoelectric performance.

title: Si-substituted MAX phases and in situ formation of Si-coated MXene composites via chlorosilane etching
abstract: A general vapor-phase etching strategy breaks thermodynamic constraints, enabling synthesis of elusive Si-substituted MAX phases (M n +1 SiX n ) with tunable A-site vacancies and one-step fabrication of Si-coated MXene composites.

title: Layers and phase identification of h- and m-GaTe via second harmonic generation
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) GaTe is a recently synthesized III–VI semiconductor that can crystallize in either a hexagonal (h) phase or a monoclinic (m) phase, and these two phases show clearly different electronic and optical anisotropies.

title: Large-scale synthesis of zinc oxide-supported indium single-atom catalysts for efficient electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction reaction
abstract: We report the large-scale synthesis of ZnO-supported In single-atom catalysts using a micro-impinging stream reactor. The catalysts achieve high formate selectivity (85%) and outstanding long-term stability (>40 h) toward CO 2 reduction reaction.

title: Thermodynamically guided synthesis of 3R-TaSe 2 nanocrystals and their superconducting behavior
abstract: Substrate-mediated CVT growth enables highly crystalline 3R-TaSe 2 nanocrystals exhibiting charge density wave behavior at ∼100 K and superconductivity below 2.2 K.

title: Emerging strategies for controllable mechanical exfoliation of crystalline thin films and nanomembranes
abstract: Emerging mechanical lift-off technology enables scalable, high-precision production of crystalline membranes for advanced heterostructures and device platforms.

title: Dual-fullerene-confined single transition metal atoms as high-efficiency catalysts for hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: DFT calculations were used to screen 26 dual-fullerene-supported TM single-atom catalysts, identifying seven candidates with exceptional HER performance.

title: Manganese-based cathode materials for aqueous zinc-ion batteries: a mini review
abstract: This review methodically covers Mn-based cathodes for AZIBs, emphasizing diverse crystal phases, energy storage mechanisms, and optimisation strategies. Additionally, it delineates prospective outlooks on AI-driven design and industrialization.

title: Site-selective functionalization of solution-processed transition metal dichalcogenides for vertical and lateral covalent networks and heterostructures
abstract: We survey covalent functionalization of solution-processed transition metal dichalcogenides through the lens of predominant basal-plane versus edge binding, and show how these chemistries enable covalent vertical and lateral (hetero)structures.

title: Who needs an electrical back-contact after all?
abstract: We report on a transformative method for performing electrical AFM modes that eliminates the need for physical back-contact. Here, we investigate remote contacts using a reconfigurable e-beam for non-destructive nanoscale characterization.

title: Effect of synthesis method on the structural properties and magnetic behavior of LnFeO 3 (Ln = La, Nd, Sm, and Gd) nanoparticles
abstract: Rare-earth orthoferrite (LnFeO 3 Ln = La, Nd, Sm, Gd) nanoparticles were synthesized by thermal decomposition and sol–gel methods. The synthesis route strongly affects particle size and their structural and magnetic properties.

title: Bridging molecular and nano-scale kinetic regimes in the crystallization of icosahedral gold nanoparticles
abstract: During the reduction of the Au( iii )–oleylamine system to 2 nm gold nanoparticles, the kinetics abruptly switch from a first-order to a zero-order regime once stable three-layer icosahedral intermediates form.

title: Copper–verteporfin coordination nanoparticles to reverse ferroptosis resistance in pancreatic cancer therapy
abstract: A novel metalloporphyrin-based nanoplatform is developed by a simple coordination method for co-delivery of a YAP inhibitor and a ferroptosis inducer to overcome ferroptosis resistance.

title: CrossMicroNet: a cross-scale small-sample image restoration framework for two-dimensional material microscopy imaging
abstract: High-quality microscopy is central to resolving the growth behavior, morphology, lattice organization and defect landscape of two-dimensional (2D) materials.

title: Ultra-small luminescent copper nanocluster-catalyzed C–S cross coupling reactions: the effect of ligand substituents in modulating catalytic efficiency
abstract: 2-Mercaptobenzimidazole-capped ultra-small luminescent copper nanoclusters (CuNCs) act as a heterogeneous catalyst for C–S cross coupling reactions with high functional group tolerance and good yields across a wide range of aryl iodides and thiols.

title: MXene–silk fibroin hybrid films with synergistic conductive networks for efficient EMI shielding and thermal management
abstract: Hydrogen-bonded interfacial coupling between Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene and SF fibers forms a conductive network in the composite membrane, enabling EMI SE >40 dB and stable photothermal/electrothermal performance.

title: Albumin coating of magnetic nanoparticles for imaging, tracking and delivery through biological barriers
abstract: Multiscale validation of BSA-coated magnetic nanoparticles as a theranostic system for crossing biological barriers.

title: Correction: Phase-engineered bismuth-rich oxybromides (Bi x O y Br z ) for visible-light photocatalytic degradation of emerging pollutants and harmful algal blooms
abstract: Correction for ‘Phase-engineered bismuth-rich oxybromides (Bi x O y Br z ) for visible-light photocatalytic degradation of emerging pollutants and harmful algal blooms’ by A. Anjitha et al. , Nanoscale , 2026, 18 , 3419–3432, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR05038C.

title: Size-dependent two-photon absorption and ultralow optical-limiting response in atomically-thin rhodonite
abstract: 2D rhodonite shows strong size-dependent NLO behavior, with enhanced 2PA and an ultralow OL threshold. Dimensional reduction strengthens orbital transitions, making it a promising material for next-generation photonic technologies.

title: A reduced NiGraf metal organic alloy in the hydrogenation of nitrobenzene to aniline: a computational analysis
abstract: DFT calculations aimed at investigating the catalytic activity and stability of the reduced form of NiGraf in mediating nitrobenzene hydrogenation to aniline unveil a reaction mechanism that explains also the catalyst's remarkable stability.

title: Self-replication of DNA cross-tile patterns from temperature-selected species
abstract: A temperature-selective DNA nanoassembly system inspired by environmental selection, in which structural patterns defined by temperature are stably inherited through replication, offers a route toward adaptive artificial nanostructures.

title: Multi-peak single object emission of MAPbBr 3 nanoplatelets synthesised using a non-template ligand-assisted reprecipitation route
abstract: Ultra-thin square-shaped MAPbBr 3 perovskite nanoplatelets (NPLs) were synthesised, using a modified ligand-assisted reprecipitation synthesis route, which exhibited multiple emission peaks for individual NPLs in single-dot spectroscopy.

title: Multifunctional MXene/polymer architectures for energy storage and wearable technologies
abstract: MXene/polymer composites combine high electrical conductivity and redox activity of MXenes with the flexibility and mechanical stability of polymers, resulting in enhanced charge storage performances.

title: Influence of composition–phase interplay on the electrochemical activity of ternary transition metal dichalcogenides
abstract: Enhanced electrochemical activity in ternary metal chalcogenides via electronic structure and phase modulation.

title: All-optical tuning of dielectric metasurfaces infiltrated with dye-doped liquid crystals
abstract: All-optical tuning of dielectric metasurfaces using liquid crystals enables reversible, spatially controlled modulation of optical resonances for ultrathin multifunctional photonics.

title: Electron-doping-induced destabilization of the dimerized insulating state in monolayer IrTe 2
abstract: External electron doping provides an effective route to control instability-driven phase transitions in monolayer IrTe 2 .

title: Co-designing growth factors for the synthesis of (6, 5)-enriched single-walled carbon nanotube horizontal arrays
abstract: Utilizing machine learning to enhance the understanding of the carbon nanotube growth dynamics, facilitating the transition from the synthesis of powdered carbon nanotubes to chirality-enriched growth of horizontally aligned carbon nanotube arrays.

title: Engineered MXene as An Analytical Tool for Health Risk Assessment of Heavy Metals
abstract: Two-dimensional MXenes are attractive materials because of their metallic conductivity, abundant surface terminations, and high aspect ratio that can be coupled with organic molecules and magnetic structures to produce multifunctional...

title: Synthesis of YIG and Bi-YIG nanoparticles by coprecipitation and thermal treatment: towards magnetic liquids with controlled magneto-optical properties
abstract: This article describes the synthesis of magnetic fluids based on well-crystallized YIG and Bi-YIG. Magneto-optical measurements have confirmed the optical quality of the ferrofluid obtained, paving the way for its integration into optical isolators.

title: Aggregation-Induced Emissive Copper(I) Clusters: From Photophysical Mechanisms to Photocatalytic Performance
abstract: Atomically precise copper clusters are an emerging class of functional materials with considerable promise in photocatalysis and luminescence. However, integrating strong emission and high catalytic efficiency within a single robust...

title: Bimetal chalcogenide TM 2 TM′X 4 monolayers: stable room temperature ferromagnetic semiconductors
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) ferromagnetic semiconductors (FMSs), which combine ferromagnetism and semiconducting properties, have long been highly desired in the field of spintronics.

title: Novel liquid–liquid interface deposition method for thin films of two-dimensional solids
abstract: A novel liquid–liquid interface method for producing thin films and van der Waals heterostructures of two-dimensional (2D) materials is presented, offering broad applicability to 2D materials held in surfactant-stabilised aqueous suspensions.

title: Anisotropy deformation behavior of multi-layer black phosphorus
abstract: Black phosphorus crystals, an exceptional two-dimensional van der Waals semiconductor, exhibit pronounced anisotropic deformation behavior under in-plane and out-of-plane loading during indentation.

title: Dopant-free synergy of Fe gradient and Na vacancies for boosted Na + transport in O3-type cathodes
abstract: Regulating radial Fe gradient and Na vacancies triggers TM layer adaptation, suppressing Fe migration, widening spacing, and boosting cathode stability and Na + efficiency.

title: Intra-interlayer competition: a key regulator for sliding ferroelectricity in hydrogen-functionalized group-III monochalcogenide monolayer
abstract: Surface hydrogen functionalization unlocks sliding ferroelectricity in monolayer MX via bilayer reconstruction, governed by an intralayer–interlayer competition mechanism and optimized by the descriptor σ IR / d 2 .

title: Correction: Type-II WO 3 /NiOOH nanoflakes enable efficient and selective low-bias photoelectrochemical saline water-splitting reaction
abstract: Correction for ‘Type-II WO 3 /NiOOH nanoflakes enable efficient and selective low-bias photoelectrochemical saline water-splitting reaction’ by Prashant Choubey et al. , Nanoscale , 2026, 18 , 3406–3418, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR04660B.

title: The nature of trehalose–protein interactions in aqueous solutions revealed by neutron scattering
abstract: Trehalose is widely recognized for stabilising proteins under conditions that promote dehydration, denaturation, or loss of function, yet its underlying mechanisms remain elusive, although exclusion from the protein surface may be of importance.

title: Review on carbon-based materials for moisture-induced energy harvesting
abstract: This review systematically analyzes how carbon-based materials address key challenges in moisture-enabled electricity generation, providing a clear roadmap and outlook for their development path from material design to scalable applications.

title: Facile phase selective synthesis of α- and β-AgVO 3 : a comparative study of their structural, morphological and photocatalytic properties
abstract: α- and β-AgVO 3 exhibit remarkable photocatalytic activity. The superior performance of the α-phase challenges the prevailing assumption that the β-phase is more active, highlighting the critical influence of morphology on photocatalytic behavior.

title: Selenium oxide nanostructure-based electrodes for rapid and simultaneous electrochemical determination of oxalic and ascorbic acids in food matrices
abstract: An electroanalytical sensing strategy was developed for the simultaneous oxidation and quantification of ascorbic acid (AA) and oxalic acid (OA) using a SeO 2 -modified carbon paste electrode (SeO 2 @CPE) integrated with a portable potentiostat.

title: Temperature-dependent electrochemical CO 2 -to-CO conversion on the bimetallic Ti–Ag nanocluster
abstract: A Ti 5 Ag bimetallic nanocluster catalyst enables efficient CO 2 -to-CO conversion, with ∼60% FE CO and surface reaction process (COOH* → CO*) as rate-determining step (RDS).

title: One-step photochemical synthesis of gold–polymer nanohybrids with engineered morphologies and catalytic activity
abstract: A rapid one-step photochemical strategy yields either solid or hollow Au–polymer nanogels. The nanogels exhibit thermoresponsive catalytic activity, highlighting the potential of this approach for designing functional hybrid materials.

title: A one-step fabrication method for CNT–QD hybrid 3D architectures with engineered optoelectronic properties
abstract: This work demonstrates 3D-printed micropillars incorporating quantum dots (QDs) and carbon nanotubes (CNTs), where efficient FRET from QDs to CNTs is confirmed by a clear reduction in QD exciton lifetime observed in time-resolved photoluminescence.

title: DFT exploration of the sensing performance of Janus transition metal dichalcogenides (ASSe; A = Sc, Ti, Zr, Nb, Pd) monolayers for HCN, H 2 S, and NH 3 toxic gases
abstract: Janus ScSSe as promising sensor for HCN, H 2 S and NH 3 gas molecules.

title: An etching–templating dual strategy for the in situ synthesis of carbon-supported iron metaphosphate and its application as an electrocatalyst
abstract: In situ construction of lamellar Fe–HPA micelles enables their transformation into uniform iron-metaphosphate nanodots in a carbon matrix.

title: Counter-doping in two-dimensional transition-metal dichalcogenides: flipping native polarity and beyond
abstract: This review covers how intrinsic defect-driven polarity in 2D transition metal dichalcogenides can be reconfigured through counter-doping strategies, providing a unified framework and enabling CMOS, optoelectronic, and neuromorphic platforms.

title: Advances and challenges in aqueous zinc-ion batteries for extreme environmental adaptability
abstract: Strategies boosting AZIBs’ extreme-condition resilience via electrode/electrolyte/interface engineering, addressing dendrites, side reactions, and thermal instability.

title: Spontaneously polarised crystalline water ice
abstract: Spectroscopy in the vacuum ultraviolet range reveals a Stark shift in peak positions, characteristic of an intrinsic electric field in thin films of crystalline water ice.

title: Potassium-intervened copper–nickel bimetallic organic framework with a potassium hydrogen phthalate ligand for high-performance aqueous potassium-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: CuNi–KHP is synthesized via a solvothermal method (160 °C, 17 h) and fabricated into an electrode with PVDF and carbon for three-electrode testing in 6 M KOH. Achieve 1568.58 F g −1 , 76 Wh kg −1 and 812 Wh kg −1 in hybrid potassium-ion supercapacitors.

title: Nano-whiskers of Fe 2 O 3 coated with NiCo LDH grown on an iron foam substrate for overall water splitting
abstract: Transition metal-based heterostructures have attracted remarkable interest for their application in efficient water-splitting electrocatalysis.

title: Nonlinear generation control with torus metasurfaces
abstract: A toroidal dipole moment associated with bound states in the continuum in silicon torus structures is numerically demonstrated at near-infrared wavelengths. Third harmonic generation can be largely enhanced and tuned via engineering the quasi-BICs.

title: MXenes for sensing technology: from fundamental properties to diverse applications
abstract: This review explores MXene-based sensing for gas, bio, pressure/strain, acoustic, and thermal applications, highlighting how their tunable properties enable revolutionary performance across these diverse modalities.

title: Semiconductor-based combustible gas sensors for the petrochemical industry: a review
abstract: This review covers materials, mechanisms, performance parameters, and optimization strategies of semiconductor-based combustible gas sensors for the petrochemical industry, offering future perspectives for petrochemical monitoring systems.

title: Stimuli-responsive noniridescent structural color of a thin-film coating based on multilayered nanosheets preserving the interlayer space
abstract: The exfoliated nanosheets preserving multilayered structures show both intercalation and building-block capabilities. A thin film exhibiting stimulus-responsive noniridescent structural color is obtained by coating multilayered nanosheets.

title: Synergistic cobalt-MOF@rGO nanocomposites for high-performance supercapacitors and electrochemical detection of Cd 2+ ions
abstract: A pseudocapacitive binary nanocomposite (PUC6@rGO) has been developed for high performance supercapacitors and detection of Cd 2+ ions.

title: The reactivity of single magnesium nanoparticles towards corrosion and galvanic replacement
abstract: Magnesium (Mg) nanoparticles are promising for plasmonic applications due to their wide resonance range, biocompatibility, and low cost. The low reduction potential of Mg leads to high reactivity, a double-edged...

title: Recent progress in two-dimensional MXenes for the electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 : a review
abstract: MXenes for CO 2 electroreduction: preparation via fluoride and fluoride-free etching, surface functionalization and doping strategies, and enhanced C 2 + product formation.

title: Phase-engineered 1T′/2H-MoS 2 heterophase junctions for high-performance aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: In this pioneering study, two-dimensional 1T/2H-MoS 2 heterophase cathodes directly synthesized through thermal evaporation enable high-performance aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: High-entropy oxide (CaCu 3 Ti 3.85 Mn 0.05 Sn 0.1 O 12 ) as a multifunctional electrocatalyst for supercapattery and water electrolysis
abstract: High-entropy oxide (CaCu 3 Ti 3.85 Mn 0.05 Sn 0.1 O 12 ) was synthesized via a semi-wet route as a electrocatalyst for supercapattery and water electrolysis.

title: Ultrasonic-shear exfoliated multi-layer graphene flakes for enhanced triboelectric nanogenerator performance toward wearable self-powered applications
abstract: Triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs) can effectively harvest mechanical energy from the environment, offering a promising solution for a sustainable power supply in wearable electronics.

title: Engineering g-C 3 N 5 /MnCo 2 S 4 heterojunction nanocomposites for highly efficient visible-light photocatalytic dye degradation and electrochemical energy conversion
abstract: Multifunctional nanomaterials that address environmental pollution and clean energy needs are highly promising, cost-effective bifunctional materials for wastewater remediation and sustainable hydrogen evolution.

title: The boosted adsorption of uranium( vi ) on phosphorylated Fe 3 O 4 @MnO 2 @UiO-66-(OH) 2
abstract: In this study, a phosphorylated Fe 3 O 4 @MnO 2 @UiO-66-(OH) 2 magnetic nanocomposite (FMU-P) was synthesized by a hydrothermal method and post-synthetic modification.

title: Conductivity-enhanced thick hole-transporting layers via doping–crosslink synergy for efficient and stable NIR QLEDs
abstract: Small-molecule-doped crosslinkers enable robust thick hole-transport layers that suppress micro-shorts and markedly enhance the operational stability of near-infrared QLEDs.

title: Harnessing scanning probe lithography for integrated photonics with anisotropic materials
abstract: Using scanning probe lithography a universal prototyping method is obtained. Employing the approach, waveguides are created in high index layered GaInS 3 . Preserved crystallinity enabled visible-light waveguiding where traditional FIB methods fail.

title: Electrospinning ultrafine graphene nanofibers for flexible sensors
abstract: This work reports a diameter control method for electrospun graphene fibers and prepares graphene nanofiber non-woven fabric with fiber diameter as fine as 66 nm. This non-woven fabric exhibits good application prospects in human motion monitoring.

title: Upconverting mixed emitter nanocomposites as sensitive luminescent thermometers within plant-inspired artificial fliers
abstract: Nanocomposites built on upconverting lanthanide-doped materials integrated into plant-inspired artificial seeds monitor the temperature of topsoil with a high signal-to-noise ratio and high sensitivity.

title: Biodegradable poly(vinyl alcohol) films reinforced with zinc oxide-functionalized carbon nanosheets derived from Punica granatum peels for sustainable food packaging
abstract: The escalating environmental impact of non-biodegradable plastic packaging has accelerated the development of sustainable and active food packaging materials with enhanced mechanical and antimicrobial performance.

title: Polymorphic phase engineering of flat plasmons in a correlated oxide
abstract: Flat plasmons are manipulated by polymorphism via epitaxial stabilization in a correlated oxide Ti 2 O 3 .

title: Improving the performance of a triboelectric nanogenerator by tuning the work function of a surface modified tribonegative polymer composite
abstract: Performance of a triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG) is influenced by numerous parameters including device structure, surface charge density, and contact area of the contacting surfaces.

title: Titanium diboride–derived nanosheet coatings for enhanced condensation heat transfer
abstract: Enhancing condensation-based heat transfer by employing titanium diboride-derived nanosheet coatings on copper surfaces.

title: Recent advancements in carbon quantum dots and polymer composites: emerging applications and future perspectives
abstract: This critical review provides a concise summary of carbon quantum dot (CQD)–polymer composites, highlighting their synthesis methods and synergistic properties and emphasising emerging applications, recent advancements and future prospects.

title: Chiral (BiO) 2 CO 3 catalysts with spin-selective charge transport enhance photocatalytic oxygen evolution
abstract: Intrinsically chiral (BiO) 2 CO 3 leverages the CISS effect to polarize photogenerated holes, promoting the 4-electron O 2 pathway while suppressing H 2 O 2 formation. This work links structural chirality to spin polarization and catalytic efficiency.

title: Near-infrared photovoltaic gating enables polarity-reconfigurable WSe 2 phototransistors for in-sensor computing
abstract: A substrate-engineered WSe 2 /HfO 2 /Si PN phototransistor enables programmable positive, negative, and zero photoconductance under near-infrared light for reconfigurable in-sensor neuromorphic computing.

title: Modeling β-sheet breaker peptides across multiple resolutions: from neurological targets to liposomal membranes
abstract: Certain β-sheet breaker peptides can target both Aβ and α-synuclein; MD simulations show PEGylated liposome adsorption via phosphate interactions, with cholesterol regulating binding and insertion.

title: Polarization-selective, tailorable, and flexible InP nanofilm UV photodetectors
abstract: Ultrathin InP nanofilms down to 5 nm thickness show tunable absorption and photoluminescence, and as photodetectors exhibit ultralow dark current, fast response, polarization-selective UV detection, and excellent flexibility for wearable devices.

title: Impact of 2D layered VS 2 on the electrochemical properties of gel polymer electrolytes for sodium-ion battery applications
abstract: In the present investigation, we synthesized composite gel polymer electrolytes (CGPEs) based on a poly(vinylidene fluoride- co -hexafluoropropylene) (PVDF-HFP)/poly(methyl methacrylate) (PMMA) blend incorporated with 2D layered vanadium disulfide.

title: Do the halide ions exchange and mix the same way in fresh and aged CsPbI 3 –CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystal mixtures?
abstract: Halide ion migration and exchange between CsPbI 3 and CsPbBr 3 .

title: Resolving ionic spectra of lead-halide perovskites to the nanometer
abstract: Low-frequency spectroscopy based on phase-modulated electric force microscopy reveals stark variations in the ionic response across triple-cation perovskite thin films, down to a nanometer resolution.

title: Osteopontin-loaded nanoarchaeosomes for enhanced osteogenesis and bone regeneration in osteoporotic zebrafish models
abstract: Graphical abstract demonstrating the osteogenic potential of NAO in vitro and in zebrafish osteporotic model.

title: Correction: Functionalized boron nanosheets as an intelligent nanoplatform for synergistic low-temperature photothermal therapy and chemotherapy
abstract: Correction for ‘Functionalized boron nanosheets as an intelligent nanoplatform for synergistic low-temperature photothermal therapy and chemotherapy’ by Zi Fu et al. , Nanoscale , 2020, 12 , 14739–14750, https://doi.org/10.1039/D0NR02291H.

title: Confinement-modulated phase transition of Fe–Ni melts in carbon nanotubes
abstract: Fe–Ni nanowires exhibit excellent magnetic properties, making them promising candidates for spintronic and high-density storage applications.

title: Co/ZnO/C Heterostructured Nanowires Derived from Layered Hydroxides: Magnetically Recyclable Photocatalysts for Efficient Tetracycline Degradation
abstract: Preparation of highly active and stable photocatalysts for tetracycline degradation remains a significant challenge. Herein, we report the synthesis of Co/ZnO/C heterostructured nanowires (NWs) through the pyrolysis of salicylate anions...

title: On the role of cation–DNA interactions in surface-assisted DNA lattice assembly
abstract: Surface-assisted DNA lattice assembly is known to be affected by the employed cation species. By combining molecular dynamics simulations and experiments, clear evidence is presented that this cation dependence is rooted in cation–DNA binding.

title: Negatively charged Janus membranes with robust pore anti-wettability for stable membrane distillation of seawater
abstract: Negatively charged Janus membranes with robust pore anti-wettability facilitate stable membrane-distillation seawater desalination with enhanced humic-acid resistance.

title: Enhancing oxygen reduction reaction activity in ZIF-derived catalysts through thermal oxidation-induced micropore enlargement
abstract: A pre-thermal oxidation of zeolitic imidazolate frameworks (ZIF) precursors yields highly efficient Co-N-C electrocatalysts with expanded mesopores, maximizing active site accessibility to boost oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) performance.

title: Purified molybdenite encapsulated in N-doped carbon nanofibers as binder-free anodes for flexible lithium-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: Purified molybdenite is electrospun with polyacrylonitrile (PAN) and carbonized to form MoS 2 @CNF, which is subsequently assembled with a CNF@AC cathode to construct a binder-free flexible lithium-ion hybrid capacitor.

title: Polymeric-protein-MOF nanoparticles with stimuli-responsive disassembly and highly reproducible synthesis
authors: 'Justin Van Houten', 'Sarah E. S. Quail', 'Melissa C. D'Amaral', 'Kezia E. Suryoraharjo', 'Abigail Richards', 'Ruben Castillo Barberi', 'Rachel Leigh Mander', 'Alana F. Ogata'
abstract: This work explores Cu- or Fe-doped BSA@ZIF-8 nanoparticles that release protein and trigger ROS-mediated cytotoxicity in the presence of H 2 O 2 .

title: Controlling particle dynamics in dead-end channels via boundary effects
abstract: Coupled diffusiophoresis and diffusioosmosis enable controllable particle transport in dead-end microchannels. Solute gradients and wall zeta potential create tunable reversal regions for precise, power-free colloid manipulation in confinement.

title: Pore size engineering in covalent organic frameworks for high-performance anion exchange membranes
abstract: COF-type anion exchange membranes with different pore sizes were synthesized. This study investigated the relationship between the ion transport mechanism and pore size. The highest hydroxide-ion conductivity reached 207.1 mS cm −1 .

title: Enhancing crystallization and mechanical properties of PET polyester using functionalized carbon nanotubes
abstract: This study presents an innovative electrochemical peeling–APTES silanization synergistic strategy for the preparation of a CNT–APTES nucleating agent that effectively integrates rigidity and flexibility.

title: Boron doping in a carbon layer: a facile strategy to boost the electrochemical performance of Na 2 FeTi(PO 4 ) 3 @C
abstract: The scarcity of lithium resources has fueled research into sodium-ion batteries (SIBs), yet the performance of cathode materials remains a critical factor that severely limits their overall electrochemical performance.

title: Alleviating perovskite toxicity and inhibition of lead leakage for sustainable perovskite solar cells
abstract: A plant Rutin molecule as a modifier is employed to improve the perovskite film quality and suppress lead leakage.

title: Interfacial atomic orbital controlled spin-hybridization proximity effects in vdW heterostructures
abstract: This study highlights that asymmetric interfacial p z orbital contributions at Fermi level drive spin splitting and induce magnetism in non-magnetic systems.

title: A mesoporous two-dimensional cobalt-phosphonocarboxylate metal organic framework (Co-MOF) as an efficient and recyclable catalyst for solvent-free multicomponent reactions
abstract: A novel 2D Co-MOF as a potent catalyst was synthesized using combined solvothermal and sonochemical methods.

title: Electrochemical reduction of oxygen-functional-group-controlled graphene oxide for high carrier mobility
abstract: We investigate electrochemical reduction of GO under acidic and basic conditions, revealing undergoing defect-free reduction with increased CC and C–OH bonds. Electrochemically reduced epGO exhibits high carrier mobility up to 146 cm 2 V −1 s −1 .

title: Enhancing colloidal stability of anisotropic magnetic nanodiscs through mesoporous silica and P(NIPAM/MAA) copolymer coatings
abstract: We present an optimization procedure to obtain a more stable colloidal dispersion of magnetic nanodiscs in aqueous environments. A pathway to obtain silica covered magnetite nanodiscs and their subsequent coating with a polymer is described.

title: Carbon fibers with infiltrated TiO 2 nanocrystalline layers: photocatalytic performance
abstract: Successful TiO 2 infiltration into PAN fibers through Vapor Phase Infiltration (VPI) was shown, and they were converted to CFs@TiO 2 via carbonization. Used for photocatalytic dye degradation, proving VPI-derived CFs@TiO 2 as durable photocatalysts.

title: Surface functionalization of graphene-based materials: enhancing the performance and sustainability of cement composites
abstract: This Minireview highlights the recent advancements, challenges and future prospects in the field of the application of functionalized graphene-family materials in cement composites.

title: From batch to flow plasmon catalysis: revealing mass transport limits in Au@Pd nanocatalysts for Suzuki coupling
abstract: Transitioning plasmonic chemistry from batch to flow, we show that controlled mass transport in a flow reactor unlocks hot-carrier transfer in gram-scale Au@Pd–Al 2 O 3 catalysts, tripling Suzuki coupling and enabling scalable light-driven synthesis.

title: First-principles modeling of electrostatics and transport in 2D topological transistors
abstract: A physically rigorous first-principles transistor modelling framework was developed for 2D topological transistors using density functional theory. I D – V G characteristics were obtained by using electrostatics and the Landauer–Büttiker formula.

title: Designing atomically precise and robust covalent organic frameworks for enhanced iodine/iodate uptake: structures with and without phenol hydroxy groups
abstract: Iodine/iodide/iodate represents a highly recalcitrant environmental pollutant, whose bioaccumulation poses significant threats to human health.

title: Fabrication of biocompatible plasmonic sensing platforms via direct growth of metal nanomaterials on modified silk films
abstract: Combining silk with noble metal nanomaterials offers a promising route to create biocompatible plasmonic platforms for biological and chemical sensing.

title: An ultrafast self-powered semi-transparent pyro-phototronic device array based on room-temperature amorphous In 2 S 3 /NiO heterostructures
abstract: Room-temperature amorphous In 2 S 3 /NiO heterostructures enable a semi-transparent, self-powered pyro-phototronic device with ultrafast (<20 μs) response and stable zero-bias operation for transparent, wearable optoelectronics.

title: Reticular assembly of 1D and 2D Cu 10 cluster-assembled materials as a recyclable luminescent probe for highly sensitive creatinine detection
abstract: Dimension-engineered Cu 10 cluster frameworks offer robust, recyclable luminescent responses for sensitive creatinine sensing in water.

title: An atomic level surface fabricated using a developed polisher for martensitic stainless steel using novel chemical mechanical polishing
abstract: An atomic model of the mechanism of chemical mechanical polishing for martensitic stainless steel.

title: Esterification synthesis of iron oxide nanoparticle tracers for magnetic particle imaging (MPI)
abstract: Esterification of iron oleate with oleyl alcohol is an alternative synthesis method for producing iron oxide tracers for magnetic particle imaging (MPI).

title: Polyethersulfone films as promising biomaterials for reducing granuloma formation and preserving tissue compatibility
abstract: The formation of a subcutaneous granuloma 4 weeks after the injection of talc and mitigation of the granuloma in the group treated with films after 4 weeks. The image was generated using BioRender.

title: From chain to framework: atomically precise silver cluster-assembled architectures
abstract: This study reveals that ligand-directed assembly enables Ag 12 nanoclusters to form 1D–3D frameworks with preserved structures, where the 1D framework shows the best catalytic performance in nitroaromatic hydrogenation.

title: Quantum nanomaterials – emerging platforms for next-generation quantum science and technology
abstract: Yujeong Bae, Paola Ceroni, and Yi Chen introduce the Nanoscale and Nanoscale Advances themed collection on ‘Quantum nanomaterials – emerging platforms for next-generation quantum science and technology’.

title: Comparison of magnetic nanoparticle immobilization methods using MPS and MRX
abstract: This work demonstrates the importance of choosing the proper immobilization method to provide reliable and reproducible magnetic nanoparticle reference samples with defined magnetic properties.

title: Liposome purification from micromolar protein background using diffusiophoretic trapping
abstract: Diffusiophoretic trapping in a salt gradient is used to purify colloids from a complex solution. A Pluronic coating prevents protein adsorption while supporting diffusiosomosis, enabling liposome capture in complex samples like plasma.

title: Prospects for high-valent nickel oxides as next-generation cathodes in primary alkaline batteries
abstract: This review offers a comprehensive discourse on prominent advances achieved in the development of high-valent nickel oxide cathodes and strategies to tune critical nanoscale phenomena to realise superior performance in primary alkaline batteries.

title: Heteroatom-engineered black phosphorus nanosheets with ambient stability for selective NO 2 /NO detection
abstract: Te and Sb doping suppress O 2 adsorption and reduce P x O y growth on BP nanosheets, leading to a thinner oxidation layer, improved ambient stability, and enhanced NO 2 /NO sensing performance.

title: Synchronizing carrier extraction and dielectric coupling in a hierarchical homometallic plasmonic catalyst for light-driven nitrate reduction
abstract: A hierarchical homometallic plasmonic Au L–S catalyst enables precise dielectric matching for optimal carrier extraction and plasmon coupling capabilities towards light-driven plasmonic catalysis of nitrate reduction into ammonia.

title: Tracking single-molecule ferritin reassembly and disassembly using polymer-coated nanopores
abstract: This study uses polymer-coated SiN x nanopores and individual-event data analysis to track real time volume and shape changes of ferritin fragments during reassembly and disassembly, offering a powerful method for probing dynamic protein processes.

title: Expression of concern: Null current hysteresis for acetylacetonate electron extraction layer in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Null current hysteresis for acetylacetonate electron extraction layer in perovskite solar cells’ by Abd. Rashid bin Mohd Yusoff et al. , Nanoscale , 2016, 8 , 6328–6334, https://doi.org/10.1039/C5NR06234A.

title: Design of a 2D/1D Bi 2 MoO 6 /Bi 19 S 27 Br 3 direct Z-scheme heterojunction with a built-in internal electric field for enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction performance
abstract: Morphology-modulated interface engineering in a 2D/1D Bi 2 MoO 6 /Bi 1 9S 27 Br 3 (BMO/BSBr) direct Z-scheme heterojunction significantly enhances charge separation and boosts photocatalytic CO 2 reduction performance.

title: Light-activated self-thermophoretic Janus nanopropellers
abstract: Light-activated Au–SiO 2 Janus nanoparticles demonstrate tunable self-thermophoretic propulsion, overcoming Brownian diffusion through optical excitation—a minimal, fuel-free system to study active matter at the nanoscale.

title: (Sub-)microscale structuring of cellulose thin films using a polymer brush-assisted microcontact printing (PolyBrushMiC) routine
abstract: We present a method for directly (sub-)micropatterning cellulose thin films using microcontact printing with a polymer-brush stamp carrying reactive imidazole groups.

title: Quantifying selective metabolite transport for the bacterial microcompartment from Haliangium ochraceum with molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: Bacterial microcompartments (BMCs) are protein-based organelles found in diverse bacteria that encapsulate reactions. Through a combination of approaches, we determine permeability for products and reactants.

title: Hierarchical 3D Fe-doped Bi 2 MoO 6 arrays supported on a Ni foam: an effective electrocatalyst for alkaline water splitting
abstract: Fe-doped Bi 2 MoO 6 3D arrays were grown in situ on a 3D conductive Ni foam via a solvothermal route. The BMOF-5 catalyst exhibits overpotentials of 262 mV for OER and 153 mV for HER at a current density of 10 mA cm −2 .

title: Separation before function: mechanistic criteria for enzyme-like activity in iron-based nanomaterials and minerals
abstract: This work establishes “separation before function” as a mechanistic criterion for enzyme-like activity in iron nanomaterials, distinguishing true catalysis from sorption and guiding future environmental and catalytic studies.

title: Transfer learning-enabled discovery of MXenes as efficient anchoring materials for Li–S batteries
abstract: A novel transfer learning framework accelerates the discovery of high-performance MXene electrocatalysts for Li–S batteries.

title: High capture capacity of CO 2 in a nickel intercalated Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene–fluorohectorite clay heterostructure
abstract: MXene–clay heterostructure with Ni(OH) 2 in the interlayer captures CO 2 , with residual 30% of CO 2 after desorption. Heterostructure enlarges available pressure window compared to pure MXene.

title: Tungsten-based ternary catalysts for selective growth of single-walled carbon nanotubes
abstract: Tungsten-containing ternary catalysts stabilize nanoparticle size and enable selective growth of small-diameter single-walled carbon nanotubes.

title: Interplay of stoichiometry and crystallization kinetics in vapor-based hybrid perovskite film growth
abstract: Vapor deposition bypasses solvent limits for precise perovskite growth. We connect stoichiometry with crystallization kinetics across high/low vacuum and vapor-assisted routes, clarifying phases, defects, and scalable process windows.

title: FeCo bimetallic phosphide heterostructure for hydrogen production via sulfion oxidation assisted alkaline seawater splitting
abstract: Nanoporous FeCo bimetallic phosphides with an FeP/Co 2 P heterostructure were constructed as bifunctional electrocatalysts for hydrogen production via sulfion oxidation assistance in alkaline seawater.

title: Correction: Dendrimers meet zwitterions: development of a unique antifouling nanoplatform for enhanced blood pool, lymph node and tumor CT imaging
abstract: Correction for ‘Dendrimers meet zwitterions: development of a unique antifouling nanoplatform for enhanced blood pool, lymph node and tumor CT imaging’ by Zhijuan Xiong et al. , Nanoscale , 2017, 9 , 12295–12301, https://doi.org/10.1039/C7NR03940A.

title: Nitro functionalization and nanoscale confinement enable ether-based quasi-solid electrolytes with stable lithium metal and high-voltage compatibility
abstract: NO 2 -functionalized UiO-66 nanochannels reshape a DME-based electrolyte into a compact CIP/AGG-rich solvation structure, enabling >5.0 V stability, stable Li plating/stripping, and high-voltage NCM811 compatibility.

title: Geometric notch-manipulated skyrmion dynamics for reconfigurable diode and logic gates in synthetic antiferromagnetic nanotrack
abstract: Schematic of the SAF nanotrack structure with tilted-boundary geometric notches for reconfigurable functionalities. Threshold current density of a single skyrmion in bidirectional motion depends on two characteristic distances of structural notches.

title: On the synthesis of chiral gold nanorods
abstract: Chiroptical active gold nanorods of diverse morphologies are synthesized from a single type of achiral gold nanorod and evaluated when exposed to relevant neuron cell lines.

title: Correction: Aqueous dispersion of carbon nanotubes by chlorine dioxide oxidation
abstract: Correction for ‘Aqueous dispersion of carbon nanotubes by chlorine dioxide oxidation’ by Yuki Itabashi et al. , Nanoscale , 2026, 18 , 5805–5811, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR05444C.

title: Nanoscale, temporal temperature mapping in AlN/GaN HEMTs via ab initio phonon Monte Carlo simulation
abstract: Via ab initio calculations and Monte Carlo methods we have shown that an accurate description of material interfaces is necessary to fully capture and describe interfacial thermal transport in semiconductors.

title: Exploring Channelrhodopsin-2 and Gold Nanoclusters Interaction: a Route to Control the Protein Photocycle
authors: 'Roberto Messina', 'Luca Bellucci', 'Stefano Corni', 'Lucia Cascino', 'Ivan Rivalta', 'Stefania D'agostino', 'Laura Zanetti-Polzi'
abstract: Hybrid light-matter platforms that combine biomolecular photochemistry with engineered electromagnetic fields offer a promising, largely unexplored way to extend optogenetic control beyond the limits of native chromophores. Channelrhodopsin-2 (ChR2), a...

title: Structural and electrical properties of solid-source MBE-grown graphene/Ge(001) heterostructures
abstract: We report the structural and electrical properties of transfer-free monolayer graphene (Gr) directly grown on Ge(001) by SSMBE, focusing on surface homogeneity and Gr/Ge interface quality, two key factors governing optoelectronic device performance.

title: Unravelling the amplified spontaneous emission mechanism in three-dimensional metal halide perovskites
abstract: MAPbI 3 thin films exhibit light absorption, exciton dissociation, and spontaneous emission, ultimately enabling stimulated emission through population inversion.

title: Real-time visualization of plasmonic nanoparticle growth dynamics by high-speed atomic force microscopy
abstract: Real-time high-speed AFM movies capture single plasmonic nanoparticle nucleation and growth, revealing growth dynamics and offering new insight into formation mechanisms.

title: Clinical translation of afterglow luminescence imaging in precision surgery of tumors: opportunities and challenges
abstract: Afterglow luminescence imaging in tumor surgery.

title: From photocorrosion to photoactivity: sulfite-gated self-etching of BiVO 4 into a black Bi–O phase
abstract: Photocorrosion is redefined as a programmable activation pathway: sulfite anions gate the light-driven reconstruction of BiVO 4 into a black, oxygen-vacancy-rich Bi–O network, enabling high-performance oxide photoanodes by corrosion engineering.

title: Balancing interfacial properties and porosity in multiscale porous carbon composite coatings for advanced Li–S battery separators
abstract: A hierarchical rGO/MPC-coated separator balances porosity and surface functionality to suppress polysulfide shuttling, enhance ion transport, and achieve high capacity retention in Li–S batteries under lean electrolyte and high sulfur loading.

title: A nanoscale view on oligo(ethylene glycol) self-assembled monolayer hydration
abstract: Nanoscale analysis of amorphous OEG SAMs by AFM-IR revealed helical domains surrounded by amorphous regions and conformational changes upon hydration at high relative humidity (RH%). Also differently hydrogen-bonded water populations were found.

title: A surfactant-mediated Ni/MgAl bifunctional catalyst for highly selective tandem conversion of acetone to methyl isobutyl ketone
abstract: A surfactant-assisted impregnation strategy was developed to construct a heterogeneous catalyst of Ni/MgAl-SDBS with coexistence of Ni 0 /Ni 2+ species, which efficiently transformed acetone into methyl isobutyl ketone with a high selectivity of 94.5%.

title: Anti-plasma adhesion peptides from one-bead one-compound technique for drug delivery
abstract: Peptides screened through one-bead one-compound technique to avoid the adsorption of proteins in the bloodstream for efficient drug delivery.

title: Dual-interfacial engineering via LiPF 6 treatment: eradicating Li 2 CO 3 on LLZTO and regulating SEI for high-voltage solid-state batteries
abstract: LiPF 6 -modification formed an in situ Li 3 PO 4 -LiF coating, leading to an increase in ionic conductivity and Li + transfer number, and inducing a dense LiF-Li 3 PO 4 -Li 3 N SEI, thereby realizing the uniform Li + deposition in high-performance ASSLBs.

title: Understanding surface wettability: insights from experiments, molecular simulations, and first-principles theory
abstract: This review discusses wettability concepts, applications, theory, experiments, and atomistic simulations, identifies key knowledge gaps and limitations, and proposes a roadmap to guide future research in designing functional surfaces. This figure was created using Google Gemini ( via Google Cloud).

title: Engineering non-linear mechanical advantage in DNA nanostructures: a wireframe adaptation of a lamina emergent mechanism
abstract: Lamina emergent mechanism principles translated to DNA origami create a lever with non-linear mechanical advantage that achieves sophisticated mechanical behavior through sinple geometric configurations.

title: Quantum dot or not? The effect of ligands on the nature of excitations in CdS nanoparticles
abstract: We investigate using many-body perturbation theory the optical and electronic properties of neutral cadmium sulfide zinc blende nanoparticles capped with (organic) ligands.

title: High-throughput thickness analysis of 2D materials enabled by intelligent image segmentation
abstract: The AI model predicts nanoscale surface features directly from optical microscopy images, enabling fast and non-destructive material characterization without the need for atomic force microscopy. Scale bar: 10 µm.

title: High-resistance-state tunneling in 25 nm TiO x /Y-doped HfO 2 /Pt nanocrossbar ferroelectric tunnel junctions
abstract: Nanocrossbar ferroelectric tunnel junctions (FTJs) with TiO x /Y-doped HfO 2 /Pt were fabricated at 25 nm. Direct tunneling was observed in the high-resistance state, achieving a TER ratio of 2.2 × 10 3 for non-volatile memory for CMOS back-end integration.

title: Rational design of open hollow nanoboxes via Ru and B synergistic electronic modulation in cobalt phosphide for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: The graphical abstract visually summarizes the synthesis process, hollow nanobox architecture, synergistic electronic effects of Ru and B, and the superior OER performance, providing an immediate overview of the study's novelty and findings.

title: High-valence Ir single atoms enhance oxygen evolution electrocatalysis
abstract: High-valence Ir single atoms were anchored on a NiS 2 support, which exhibited superior OER catalytic activity by lowering the energy barrier and accelerating the reaction kinetics in the OER process.

title: Interplay of hot carrier relaxation and trapping in red emissive formamidinium lead iodide perovskite nanorods
abstract: Stable, red-emissive formamidinium lead triiodide (FAPbI 3 ) nanorods have been synthesized by modification of conditions for the synthesis of FAPbI 3 nanocubes.

title: Role of weak and transient interactions in the polymer-free electrospinning of β-cyclodextrin derivatives
abstract: Polymer-free electrospinning enables the fabrication of nanofibers from low-molecular-weight compounds without polymeric additives, where intermolecular interactions or molecular aggregates substitute for polymer chain entanglements.

title: Precision ladder-structured silver cluster assembly bridged by tetrazine linkers for highly sensitive and selective luminescence recognition of amino acids
abstract: A ladder-structured silver cluster framework stabilized by tetrazine linkers enables selective and recyclable luminescence recognition of basic amino acids in water. The background image was generated using BRIA AI.

title: Unraveling the mechanism of graphene oxide-mediated disruption of protein dimers
abstract: Graphene oxide induces β-lactoglobulin dimer dissociation by preferentially adsorbing monomers, as revealed by combined spectroscopy, microscopy, and molecular dynamics simulations, elucidating nanomaterial–protein interface mechanisms.

title: A narrow-bandgap conjugated-polymer/MoS 2 hybrid photodetector for room temperature shortwave infrared detection up to 2000 nm
abstract: A PTBD/MoS 2 photodetector shows a high photoresponsivity of 2.4 × 10 5 A W −1 and a specific detectivity of 9.9 × 10 11 Jones at 1550 nm, placing it as a high-performance room-temperature SWIR photodetector better than commercialized uncooled detectors.

title: A synergistic ZnO/FAPbBr 3 nanocrystal n–n heterojunction for low-bias, fast, broadband photodetection and memristive applications
abstract: A ZnO/FAPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystal n–n heterojunction photodetector shows high on/off ratio, strong responsivity, fast switching, and excellent sensitivity under low bias—ideal for broadband imaging and sensing applications.

title: An anti-freezing, mechanically tough hydrogel via hydro-locking for mechanical sensors and flexible supercapacitors at low temperatures
abstract: Using a facile synergistic hydro-locking double-salt strategy, we synthesized the PACL 35 hydrogel, which exhibits excellent cryogenic tolerance and high ionic conductivity.

title: Phonon modulation of strongly coupled gold tetrahedral plasmonic nanoparticles and a carbocyanine J-aggregate
abstract: Gold tetrahedral nanoparticles coupled with J-aggregated TDBC dye form polaritons with Rabi splitting energies of ∼212 meV. Transient absorption spectra show that the extinction coefficient of polaritons is modulated by phonons.

title: Active site microenvironment engineering in M–N–C single-atom nanozymes: from precision regulation to mechanistic insights
abstract: This review focuses on active site microenvironment engineering of metal–nitrogen–carbon single-atom nanozymes (M–N–C SANs), highlighting their high catalytic activity, selectivity, and stability from synergistic metal–carrier effects.

title: ROS-responsive multifunctional DCS-based micelle hydrogel for simultaneously inhibiting post-resection melanoma recurrence and promoting wound healing
abstract: The ROS-responsive micelle hydrogel (AB@MH) can block post-resection melanoma recurrence and promote wound healing by selectively eradicating tumor/bacteria.

title: Superior performance of printed optoelectronic synapses based on defect-controlled monolayer MoS 2 with ultralow power consumption for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Printed optoelectronic synapses using defect-controlled monolayer MoS 2 with ultralow power consumption for neuromorphic computing.

title: Geometry-driven dimensional crossover from weak localization to superconducting fluctuations in nanostructured NbN networks
abstract: Geometrically engineered nanohole arrays in disordered NbN films enhances superconducting transport, demonstrating quantum correction and localization contributions.

title: Aluminum doping-induced α/γ-MnO₂ heterophase and oxygen vacancy defect engineering for high-performance aqueous zinc-manganese batteries
abstract: Developing cost-effective aqueous rechargeable zinc-manganese batteries with high capacity and long cycle life remains a challenge. In this study, a nanorodstructured Al-doped MnO₂ cathode material (PAMO) containing α-MnO₂/γ-MnO₂ heterophases and...

title: Perovskite solar cells towards industrialization: overcoming challenges with data-driven strategies
abstract: This review outlines the key challenges in PSC industrialization and presents an integrated ML framework to accelerate materials discovery and enable data-driven commercialization.

title: Unconventional non-monotonic oscillations of magnetic damping in a ferromagnet/non-magnet heterostructure
abstract: Unconventional oscillatory variation in magnetic damping with a change in the thickness of the magnetic layer in a ferromagnet/non-magnet heterostructure is demonstrated and correlation with the spin scattering phase space is established.

title: DNA–cisplatin modified single-walled carbon nanotubes for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A three-component catalyst material has been developed for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) from preformed DNA–cisplatin (cisPt) adducts functionalised on single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) under acidic conditions (pH 3).

title: Recent advances in inorganic catalysts for CO 2 cycloaddition reactions: catalysts, engineering strategies and mechanism
abstract: This review summarizes advances in inorganic catalysts for CO 2 cycloaddition, highlighting acid-base cooperation and strategies such as doping, defects and facet control, addressing challenges in cyclic carbonate synthesis under mild conditions.

title: Nanoscale thermocapillary flow as a recording medium for infrared absorption spectra of individual carbon nanotubes
abstract: Nonlinear thermocapillary flow encodes the optical absorption of a single carbon nanotube as a permanent trench, enabling infrared spectroscopy of heterogeneous low-dimensional nanomaterials on substrates.

title: Defect-assisted vertical proton channels in highly oriented bismuth strontium tantalum oxide nanosheet laminar films
abstract: A-site vacancy in bismuth strontium tantalum oxide nanosheet boosts out-of-plane proton conductivity. These vacancies create in-plane channels that shorten proton migration paths for the out-of-plane conductivity.

title: Experimental and theoretical overview on the origin of photoluminescence in carbon nanodots
abstract: Schematic overview of the evolution of origin of PL in CNDs including core, edge, surface, and molecular states.

title: Polydopamine-coated triamcinolone acetonide acetate nanocrystals: prevention of postoperative peritoneal adhesion by combining anti-inflammation, oxidative stress scavenging and cytokine adsorption
abstract: Prevention of postoperative peritoneal adhesion (PA) remains inadequately addressed by current clinical practice due to difficulties in precise regulation of inflammation and elimination of peroxidation products.

title: Chiral π-Conjugated Polymer Films via Kinetic-Controlled Dip-Coating for Circularly Polarized Light Information Encoding
abstract: The development of chiral organic semiconductors with efficient circularly polarized light (CPL) detection capability is crucial for advanced optoelectronic applications, such as secure communication and optical information processing. However, translating...

title: Templated Growth and Intercalation Pathways of Nickel Nanoclusters on Graphene/Ir(111)
abstract: The growth and intercalation of nickel (Ni) nanoclusters on the moiré superlattice of graphene(Gr)/Ir(111) were investigated by scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) under ultra-high vacuum (UHV) conditions. At room temperature (RT),...

title: Lattice distortion induced enhanced up-conversion luminescence from Er 3+ , Yb 3+ codoped MgAl 2 O 4 nanocrystals through Cr 3+ incorporation
abstract: Er 3+ –Yb 3+ –Cr 3+ tridoped MgAl 2 O 4 nanocrystals exhibit defect-engineered lattice distortion and enhanced up-conversion emission, enabling efficient color tuning from the white to near red region under near-infrared excitation.

title: Photo-induced and thermal bistability of spin crossover nanoparticles
abstract: Varying the core–shell lattice mismatch allows systematic control of the physical of spin crossover nanoparticles; this includes controlling a “Bordeaux relationship” between T 1/2 and T LIESST .

title: NIR-II silver chalcogenide nanocrystals: synthesis, photophysical modulation and deep-tissue fluorescence imaging
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in the synthesis, photophysical modulation, and deep-tissue NIR-II imaging applications of silver chalcogenide nanocrystals.

title: A comprehensive review on MXenes: synthesis, stability, properties and their functionalization for M-ion batteries and supercapacitors
abstract: MXene have emerged as a highly promising materials, and their distinctive layered structure and tunable surface functionalization provide exceptional properties that enhance their electrochemical performance in M-ion batteries and supercapacitors.

title: Design of melamine/ZIF-67-derivatives based Z-shaped metamaterial for ultra-broadband microwave absorption
abstract: Z-shaped 3D metamaterials exhibit outstanding performances (RL min of −20.75 dB with EAB of 12 GHz at 6.5 mm) due to the synergistic loss combining microscopic material loss and macroscopic structural consumption.

title: Moiré superstructure-dependent growth mechanisms of transition metal dichalcogenides on the Au(111) surface
abstract: The non-stoichiometric MoS 2 edges exhibit reconstructed structures depending on the chemical environment. Moiré superstructure–dependent growth of these edges, arising from lattice mismatch with substratedetermines the morphology of MoS 2 islands.

title: Regioisomer-specific, self-healing, and moldable supramolecular hydrogel fabrication: chiroptical tuning, biocompatibility, cell attachment, and bacteria-infected wound healing topical applications
abstract: Herein, we demonstrated the formation of a biocompatible hydrogel using a novel dipeptide, H–Glu–Ala–OH (or EA), for the first time. The streptomycin-loaded hydrogel showed excellent antibacterial activity and was utilized for topical wound healing.

title: Broadband microwave-absorbing GO–CNT nanocomposites enabled by synergistic FeCoNi ternary catalysis
abstract: Melamine-assisted CVD with an FeCoNi catalyst constructs a hierarchical GO–CNT framework. Oxidation tailors polarization/permeability to deliver −49.4 dB at 2.2 mm or 5.32 GHz EAB at 1.8 mm.

title: Local deformations quantified with the common sublattice method
abstract: Due to epitaxial relationship, there exists a common sublattice, which is close (< δ ) to any measured spot of either the substrate or the layer. Lattice-fitting procedure adjusts it to the measured spots without the need to know their origin.

title: Metal phosphides as efficient catalysts for methanol oxidation reaction
abstract: Transition metal phosphides enable highly active and selective liquid formate production, offering added value without gaseous emissions or catalyst poisoning.

title: Correction: Nanobody engineering for enhanced-sensitivity rapid COVID-19 tests
abstract: Correction for ‘Nanobody engineering for enhanced-sensitivity rapid COVID-19 tests’ by Eunji Jeong et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 23329–23342, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR02568K.

title: Two-dimensional Mo 3 N 2 MXene as a competitive-capacity and metallic anode for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: First-principles calculations are performed to systematically explore the electrochemical performance of 2D MXene Mo 3 N 2 as a Li-ion battery anode, focusing on impacts of surface functional groups (–O, –OH) and Mo vacancies.

title: Multiexciton absorption cross sections of CdSe@CdS nanorods studied using pump–repump–probe spectroscopy

title: Out-of-plane strain induced non-thermal bandgap tuning of black phosphorus on-chip devices
abstract: Pioneering use of out-of-plane strain (not in-plane), compatible with rigid integrated photonics, and this non-thermal tuning mechanism not only eliminating thermal management but offering unprecedented range and stability.

title: A scalable electrochemical FET immunosensor with crumpled graphene for sensitive detection of prostate cancer derived exosomes
abstract: Accurate exosome quantification is necessary for distinguishing early-stage cancer patients from malignant conditions.

title: Conduction mode transition in Ag nanowire–mesh hybrid electrodes by junction welding for high-performance transparent conductive electrodes
abstract: Junction welding induces a percolative-to-bulk conduction transition, shifting the performance-governing factor of hybrid electrodes from resistance to transmittance.
