abstract: Achieving spontaneous dipole alignment without external poling remains a grand challenge in developing high-performance ferroelectric nanogenerators. This work reports a self-poling mechanism driven by engineered interfacial fields at the polymer–ceramic...

title: Pyrazinium ionic liquid for preparing a 0D/2D hybridized N,P-codoped carbon enriched with pyridinic N and its enhanced electrocatalytic performance toward the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Pyrazine IL is utilized to prepare a pyridinic N-enriched N,P-codoped carbon material with a 0D/2D hybridized morphology, which exhibits superior electrocatalytic performance in ORR.

title: An all-photonic isolator using atomically thin (2D) bismuth telluride (Bi 2 Te 3 )
abstract: A light-rectifying fast-response all-photonic diode is realized using a 2D-Bi 2 Te 3 /2D-hBN heterostructure through a surface engineering method.

title: Controlling electron transfer channels in a plasmonic scanning tunneling microscope junction under light excitation
abstract: Control electron transfer channels in a plasmonic STM junction via tuning the current. Plasmon-assisted field emission resonances are used as indicators for electron transfer processes.

title: Advances in 3D printing for zinc-ion battery applications: a review of electrode materials and electrolytes
abstract: This review navigates the promise of zinc-ion batteries from fundamental multi-electron redox to practical, safe, and cost-effective aqueous systems for next-generation energy storage.

title: Nanostructured high-entropy oxides for catalysis: linking entropy to function
abstract: Review synthesis and nanoscale characterization to elucidate composition, structure, and property links in HEOs. Summarize applications in thermal/electro/photocatalysis and energy storage, as well as challenges for materials design and scale-up.

title: A preliminary oxidation-assisted derivation strategy for biomass to N,O co-doped carbon used in multifunctional capacitor applications
abstract: A synergistic strategy involving NaOH and H 2 O 2 is employed to successfully prepare N,O co-doped activated carbon, which exhibits excellent electrochemical performance in both supercapacitors and zinc-ion hybrid capacitors.

title: Cryo-optical setup for wide-field microscopy and spectroscopy of luminescent nanomaterials
abstract: A versatile cryo-optical setup enabling imaging, steady-state and time-resolved photoluminescence, and second-order photon correlation measurements for the characterization of quantum nanomaterials.

title: Thermally activated delayed fluorescence materials for nanomaterial-based light-emitting diodes
abstract: Hybrid systems comprising TADF materials and inorganic emitters, such as semiconductor quantum dots and perovskite nanostructures, are utilized in light-emitting diodes.

title: Phase transition of SiC support induces dispersed Na 2 WO 4 catalysts for CH 3 Cl-to-C 2 H 3 Cl conversion
abstract: Phase transition of SiC support to cristobalite induces dispersed Na 2 WO 4 catalysts for efficient CH 3 Cl-to-C 2 H 3 Cl conversion.

title: Unravelling charge carrier separation mechanism in Ag/β-BaTi 2 O 5 heterostructures for high-efficiency photocatalysis: a synergistic study combining Kelvin probe force microscopy and density functional theory
abstract: The sustainable treatment of industrial organic wastewater remains challenging due to the stabilization and aromatic structures against conventional degradation pathways.

title: Plasmon-modified dielectric Mie resonances in laser-synthesized Si@Au nanoparticles for photothermal therapy applications
abstract: Core-satellite Si@Au nanoparticles exhibit plasmon-modified Mie resonances in the near-infrared spectral region. In vitro experiments demonstrated both low toxicity and high efficiency of the Si@Au NPs as photohyperthermia sensitizers.

title: Potential of gold nanoparticles in nucleic acid amplification: from amplification enhancement to product detection
abstract: The unique properties of gold nanoparticles enhance nucleic acid-based detection by enabling photothermal heating, improving sensitivity and specificity, and offering a range of detection strategies.

title: SLPcalculator: a web-based tool to estimate nanoparticle heating with peak analysis and F -test
abstract: Accurately determining the Specific Loss Power (SLP) remains a major challenge in magnetic hyperthermia and photothermal heating. The SLPCalculator is an open access, easy-to-use and reliable tool for the SLP determination.

title: Dysprosium induced structural modulation for reduced working potential and enriched lithium storage sites
abstract: A flake-like lithium dysprosium titanate (LIDT) anode is developed to circumvent the high voltage limitation of lithium titanate. The graphical abstract image was created using Google Gemini.

title: In-plane mechanical properties of terephthalate-based two-dimensional metal–organic frameworks
abstract: Atomic force microscopy stretching of three structurally related 2D and 3D MOF nanosheets reliably measured their in-plane elastic moduli and revealed the critical role of the interlayer bonding and the coordination nodes.

title: Noble-metal-free recyclable electronic nanoinks for wireless wearable sensors
abstract: This study elucidates how functional property changes in printed sensors across multiple reuse phases and presents a surface-cleaning method for regenerating printable nanoinks.

title: First-principles investigation of transition metal doping effects on H 2 dissociative adsorption in TiFe-based alloys
abstract: This work reveals that most transition metal dopants, contrary to common expectations, do not promote H 2 adsorption on TiFe. It identifies Co and Sc as optimal dopants, which enhance performance via charge redistribution and orbital hybridization.

title: Red emissive carbon dots: synergistic interplay between core and surface states
abstract: COOH groups on as-synthesized r-CDs quench emissions. However, after column chromatography, r-CDs contain mainly –COO– surface emissive states, with SO, and –SO 2 – groups, which contribute to the observed high QY of 42% in DMSO.

title: Probing charge and dielectric relaxation in protein interactions using atomic field-effect transistor biosensors
abstract: Dual-parametric atomic biosensors enable simultaneous tracking of charge and dielectric kinetics during protein interactions in a single device.

title: Visible-light-driven antibacterial activity of poly(3-hexylthiophene-2,5-diyl) nanoparticles
abstract: Sterilisation using photocatalysts does not lead to the development of drug-resistant bacteria and has therefore attracted significant attention.

title: From AI discovery to macroscopic formula: a quasi-stability criterion for quantum dots inspired by Elon Musk's vision
abstract: An AI Supervisor integrates experimental records to derive a macroscopic stability formula for quantum dots, validated by high-throughput experiments.

title: Sustainable lithium recovery from a spent LiFePO 4 cathode via K 2 S 2 O 8 -assisted solid state oxidation through mechanochemistry
abstract: A rapid acid-free mechanochemical process using K 2 S 2 O 8 recovers ∼99.8% Li from spent LFP, yielding reusable Li 3 PO 4 , FePO 4 , and eco-friendly K 2 SO 4 .

title: Basalt fibers with surface-coated hybrid carbon nanofillers for linear temperature and pressure sensing
abstract: A synergistic surface modification strategy enables fabricating BF-based composites with a conductivity of ∼230 S m −1 , TCR of 0.18% per °C, excellent signal linearity ( R 2 > 0.999) within 30–150 °C, and linear pressure sensing within 0–0.5 MPa.

title: Silicon anodes in lithium-ion batteries: nanoscale engineering, composite strategies, and industrial prospects
abstract: A review focusing on the fundamental mechanisms of Si anodes, summarizing the latest laboratory and industry research strategies, and exploring commercialization pathways through case studies of emerging technologies and global investment.

title: Advanced gas sensors via nanoscale structure engineering and fabrication strategies
abstract: This review presents a fabrication-centered framework linking wet, dry, and hybrid strategies with nanoscale morphology, defect chemistry, and heterointerfaces, showing how these factors govern sensing performance and scalable device integration.

title: Dual Z-scheme g-C 3 N 4 /GQD/ZnIn 2 S 4 composites with enhanced photocatalytic degradation of xanthate under visible light irradiation
abstract: In situ XPS and DFT reveal a GQD-mediated dual Z-scheme in CN/GQDs/ZIS, which promotes interfacial charge separation and O 2 ˙ − generation, enabling efficient visible-light-driven xanthate degradation.

title: Effective reactive reach of plasmonic hot holes through molecular barriers
abstract: The efficiency of plasmon-driven oxidation reactions depends on the ability of plasmon-generated hot holes to reach reactive interfaces.

title: Divergent phase evolution in mixed oxide W 0.5 Mo 0.5 O 3 under electron beam irradiation and thermal annealing
abstract: In situ TEM reveals distinct transformation pathways in W 0.5 Mo 0.5 O 3 by decoupling electron irradiation effects from thermal annealing, demonstrating how differing interaction mechanisms dictate the final structure and crystallinity.

title: Recent Progress on Synthesis and Electrocatalytic Applications of Ni-Based Alloy Catalysts
abstract: Ni-based alloys represent a significant class of non-precious metal electrocatalysts, featured with tunable compositions, favorable electronic properties, and excellent chemical stability in alkaline media. Introducing a secondary element into Ni...

title: Bromine-functionalized carbazole derivatives in perovskite precursors: defect passivation for enhanced perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: Carbazole phosphonic acid additives (2PACz and Br-2PACz) incorporated into perovskite precursors significantly enhance power conversion efficiency (PCE), with Br-2PACz exhibiting higher photovoltaic performance.

title: Epitaxial growth of single-crystal violet phosphorus flakes on silicon substrates
abstract: Violet phosphorus structure, a promising van der Waals semiconductor, has attracted growing attention due to its unique properties. It is still a big challenge to grow violet phosphorus or phosphorene...

title: Realizing ultra-multi-mode high- Q plasmonic resonances by extracting guided modes via metallic lattices
abstract: By exploring exotic out-of-plane energy coupling of lattice units, this work develops a strategy to realize ultra-multi-mode high- Q plasmonic resonances.

title: Preferential outward diffusion of Ni in a CuNi alloy proliferates active sites by an order of magnitude for the OER and MOR
abstract: Here we report the selective outward diffusion of Ni in a CuNi alloy, leading to enhanced OER and MOR activities in alkaline media, emphasizing the significance of microstructural reorganization in electrocatalysis.

title: Constructing a composite catalyst containing amorphous nickel hydroxide and crystalline lanthanum carbonate hydroxide for urea electrolysis
abstract: Replacing OER with UOR can reduce the potential of Ni(OH) 2 /La 2 (CO 3 ) 2 (OH) 2 /NF by 0.29 V when driving the current density of 100 mA cm −2 , confirming the feasibility of energy-efficient hydrogen production from urea-assisted water splitting. Background generated with AI.

title: Photothermally induced defect engineering: boosting photothermal synergistic selective catalytic oxidation of benzyl alcohol over in situ oxygen vacancy-modulated ultrathin ZnTi-LDH nanosheet
abstract: Ultrathin ZnTi-LDH nanosheets with oxygen defects modulated via the photothermal synergy effect have been investigated for the photothermal oxidation of benzyl alcohol.

title: High-performance room-temperature NO 2 gas sensors enabled by 0D SnO 2 /1D WO 3 heterostructures for wearable applications
abstract: A wearable NO 2 gas sensor was fabricated based on a SnO 2 /WO 3 heterostructure. The device operates at room temperature with fast response, excellent humidity resistance, and long-term stability, enabling real-time monitoring and alarm functions.

title: Charge transport and trap state engineering in transition metal-doped bismuth vanadate photoanodes: a DFT study
abstract: Dopant-induced band-edge modulation lowers OER free-energy barriers, while enhanced active-site charge transfer improves PEC performance.

title: Correction: Layered intercalation ferroelectricity induced by asymmetric ion coordination: a mini-review
abstract: Correction for ‘Layered intercalation ferroelectricity induced by asymmetric ion coordination: a mini-review’ by Yaxin Gao et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 25477–25483, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR03854E.

title: Anti-interference bimodule biosensors with Au/Cu 2 O hollow spheres for accurate detection of uric acid in human serum
abstract: This work presents a flexible biosensor with independent pH and uric acid modules. The integrated real-time pH calibration corrects signal drift, enabling reliable uric acid detection in human serum.

title: Impact of multivalent cation exchange on the electrochemical polarization and high energy storage of copper telluride nanoparticles: a comprehensive computational study
abstract: A multi-scale theoretical investigation points to cation exchange in copper telluride frameworks as a novel strategy to improve energy storage.

title: Recent advances in atomically precise metal nanocluster-based electrocatalysts for hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Atomically precise metal nanoclusters have emerged as efficient electrocatalysts for hydrogen evolution reactions (HER). This review features the key strategies that can be employed to improve HER performance.

title: Molecular mechanism of the fracture properties of slide-ring crosslinked elastomers
abstract: The effect of crosslink density and coverage of the slide-rings on the properties and molecular mechanism of the fracture of slide-ring crosslinked elastomers is investigated by adopting a coarse-grained model.

title: MXene/biomacromolecule composites: structures, properties, fabrication and applications
abstract: Biomacromolecule-regulated MXene composites form hierarchical structures with stable transport pathways, enabling multifunctional systems for EMI shielding, actuation, biomedicine, energy storage, electronics, and water purification.

title: Electron transport through negatively curved nanographenes
abstract: We report the first electron-transport study through saddle-shaped NGs, using experimental and theoretical approaches. For the studied systems, our results show that there are no significant changes in conductance, compared with the planar analogues.

title: A ratiometric fluorescent sensor based on carbon dots encapsulated in ZIF-8 for visual discrimination and detection of tetracycline and chlortetracycline
abstract: CDs@ZIF-8 enables ratiometric fluorescence detection of TC/CTC and demonstrates low detection limits and excellent visual detection performance. A portable colorimetric sensing platform integrated with a ratiometric logic gate was developed.

title: Sub-5 nm one-dimensional post-transition-metal monochalcogenide gate-all-around MOSFETs
abstract: Sub-5 nm GAA-FETs based on MX (M = In, Ga; X = S, Se, Te) nanowires demonstrate excellent electrostatic control and suppressed short-channel effects, achieving outstanding performance. This promises a solution for beyond-Si CMOS technologies.

title: A single-element heterovalent doping strategy stabilizing the cathode structure for reversible zinc-ion storage to power soft robotics
abstract: This work stabilizes the structure and enhances the capacity of δ-MnO 2 cathodes through heterovalent Ce doping. The resulting Ce-MnO 2 achieves a high specific capacity of 374.5 mAh g −1 and excellent cycling stability over 1000 cycles, demonstrating its potential for driving soft actuator technolo…

title: Leveraging DNA-based biomaterials for advanced cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Outlining programmable DNA-based biomaterials for precise immunomodulation within tumor and lymphoid tissues, activating antigen-presenting cells and T cells to drive synergistic antitumor immunity.

title: Spectroscopic investigation of the influence of Ni particle size in CO 2 methanation
abstract: CO 2 methanation was studied on Al 2 O 3 -supported Ni catalysts with 4.6 to 8.4 nm particles. The reaction rate was found to increase with Ni particle size, accompanied by variations in surface species observed through in situ IR spectroscopy.

title: Aramid nanofiber–poly(vinyl alcohol) composite gel polymer electrolytes for lithium chloride-based supercapacitors
abstract: Functionally differentiated gel polymer electrolytes enable mechanically robust, ion-accessible, and longitudinally scalable fiber-shaped energy storage devices.

title: Reassessing the role of carbon shells in magnetite nanoparticles: a comparative adsorption study of ionic dyes
abstract: This study explores the comparative performance of carbon-coated and bare magnetite nanoparticles to evaluate the fundamental role, necessity, and true performance advantages provided by the carbon shell.

title: Nanocomposite hydrogels with temperature-accelerated antioxidant activity for ocular applications
abstract: Oxidative stress limits ocular therapy, but current systems lack stability and responsiveness. We report a pNIPAM–Ag/Cu@Cu nanocomposite that ensures stability and transparency, with temperature-accelerated scavenging via LCST-mediated contraction.

title: Compositional control of the effective magnetic anisotropy in Mn and Co substituted spinel nanoparticles
abstract: Compositional control of effective magnetic anisotropy of Fe 3− x − y Mn x Co y O 4 nanoparticles synthesized using a thermal decomposition technique.

title: Composition- and structure-tunable CoNiFe hydroxide nanostructures toward enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: CoNiFe LDH nanocones with mixed coordination are engineered via Fe incorporation and topochemical oxidation. Exfoliation to monolayer nanosheets further boosts OER activity by maximizing accessible active sites.

title: Thickness-driven phase selection for epitaxial helical tellurium on a van der Waals superconductor
abstract: We reveal a thickness-driven phase selection mechanism for Te on NbSe 2 , where a critical two-layer (2L) threshold triggers the spontaneous transformation from a kinetically trapped striped phase to the thermodynamic stable helical structure.

title: A Swiss army knife for the treatment of bone cancers: a new multifunctional platform based upon SPIONs@copper-doped bioactive glass core–shell nanoparticles
abstract: Mesoporous γ-Fe 2 O 3 @SiO 2 –CaO–CuO nanoparticles were prepared. Their potential as an MRI contrast agent and hyperthermia treatment and under the respectively tested conditions, both antibacterial effect and absence of cytotoxicity were demonstrated.

title: Silver exchange dynamics in monolayer-protected doped gold clusters
abstract: The mechanism of inter-cluster exchange reactions remains an open problem in nanoparticle chemistry.

title: Universal scaling formalism and analytical optimization criterion for multiscale geometric design of thermoelectric metamaterials
abstract: A universal scaling function g (Tr opt ) governs thermoelectric performance in constricted leg geometries, leading to Performance Design Maps and an analytical optimization rule expressed in terms of the Biot number, Bi, as g (Tr opt ) = Bi.

title: Molybdenum-driven electronic restructuring of iron carbides unlocks faster volmer kinetics in alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: Molybdenum (Mo) incorporation into Iron Carbide tunes the electronic structure and promotes in situ Fe 0 /Mo 0 formation, accelerating the Volmer step and enabling efficient, stable alkaline HER with low overpotential.

title: An AIE-active supramolecular cross-linked polymer gel based on multiple orthogonal non-covalent interactions with stimulus-responsive properties
abstract: The swelling and shrinking behavior of an AIE-active supramolecular cross-linked polymer gel BCE⊃G1 regulated by Na + and 15-crown-5.

title: Unravelling the structure of broadband white-emitting silver nanoclusters stabilized in sulfur-doped zeolites
authors: 'José Adán Moreno-Torres', 'Catarina Viola', 'Francesco D'Acapito', 'Paul Thompson', 'Andreia Ruivo', 'César A. T. Laia', 'Peter Lievens', 'Eduardo Coutiño-Gonzalez', 'Didier Grandjean'
abstract: By using XEOL–XAFS, we show that the intense broadband white-light emission of Ag/S-zeolites originate from a combination of distinct oxygen-coordinated Ag 3 O, Ag 4 O 2 , and Ag 6 O 4 nanoclusters stabilized within their sodalite cages by SO 4 2− anions.

title: Low-temperature growth of high-quality multilayer graphene films on porous CuNi alloys for enhanced corrosion resistance
abstract: Multilayer graphene (MLG) is an excellent protective material for engineering alloy in aqueous environments, owing to its high crystallinity and nm-scale thickness.

title: Cornea-SELEX-derived DNA aptamers for preparing spherical nucleic acids and corneal staining
abstract: By densely attaching corneal tissue SELEX-derived aptamers to gold nanoparticles, spherical nucleic acids were created to stain the surface of the eyes.

title: Biosynthesized iron oxide nanoparticles using Trichoderma harzianum with applications for phytopathogen control
abstract: Fungal biosynthesis as a sustainable strategy for the production of iron oxide nanoparticles with tailored physicochemical properties and applications in biological control.

title: High-adhesion stretchable organic single-crystal photoelectric thin films
abstract: Employing a cobweb-inspired network architecture, this work achieves simultaneously strong adhesion and omnidirectional stretchability in organic single-crystal films. The resulting photodetector maintains invariant functionality under deformation.

title: Deterministic mode analysis of InP plasmonic nanowire lasers
abstract: Deterministic mode identification in InP plasmonic nanowire lasers is realized by matching measured spectra with simulated effective indices, revealing mode evolution on SiO 2 , Au, and graphene/Au for reliable nanolaser design.

title: Palladium on S,N-containing carbon materials derived from covalent triazine-based frameworks (CTFs) for C–C coupling and electrocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Ionothermal synthesis of S,N-rich CTF enables reducing-agent-free formation of Pd( ii )/Pd(0), affording a robust dual-function catalyst for Suzuki–Miyaura coupling and hydrogen evolution.

title: Fluorinated Zr-MOF-modified separators for Li–S batteries with enhanced electrochemical performance

title: Simultaneous 100% PLQY of Mn 2+ emission and high Pb substitution via Sr 2+ doping in Mn-CsPbCl 3 nanocrystals
abstract: Sr 2+ doping reduces defect density and mitigates lattice distortion, thereby enhancing Mn 2+ emission and increasing the Pb substitution rate in Mn-CsPbCl 3 nanocrystals.

title: First-principles prediction of 2D non-centrosymmetric Al 2 O 3 with tunable piezoelectric and electric-field-responsive properties
abstract: Using DFT and global structure search, a new non-centrosymmetric Al 2 O 3 is predicted. The structure can be tuned by using an external electric field. These results offer a pathway toward electric-field-gated non-volatile memory devices.

title: Performance and stability evaluation of LaF 3 thin-film waveplates for high-power 266 nm laser applications
abstract: Thin-film LaF 3 waveplates exhibit superior stability and low retardance drift under changing humidity compared with all-SiO 2 waveplates, supporting reliable high-power laser operation at a 266 nm wavelength.

title: Quantifying charge carrier dynamics in fullerene-sensitized ZnO nanorod array photoanodes for enhanced photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: In this study, we employed phenyl-C 61 -butyric acid methyl ester (PCBM)-coated ZnO nanorod arrays (ZnO/PCBM) as photoanodes to enhance water splitting in the photoelectrochemical (PEC) process.

title: Designer charge-transfer van der Waals heterostructures
abstract: Programmable interlayer charge transfer in two-dimensional van der Waals heterostructures underpins a wide range of emergent quantum phenomena, ranging from excitonic dynamics and light–matter interactions to various correlated electron states.

title: Dopant-rich surface alloying effects on photoexcited carrier pathways in Cu nanoclusters with adsorbed CH 4 and CO 2
abstract: Surface alloying controls excitation in Cu nanoclusters under illumination. Ag promotes collective, delocalized excitations, while Pd and Pt induce localized charge-transfer near CH 4 , enabling dopant-dependent photoexcited carrier pathways.

title: Electrically resolving 1 nanometer semiconductor quantum wells and superlattices in photodiodes by conductive scanning probe microscopy
abstract: A high-resolution conductive scanning probe microscopy method was developed to electrically resolve 1 nm quantum wells and superlattices (SLs), revealing that SLs effectively suppress dislocation propagation and Zn diffusion.

title: In-situ Synthesis of Binder-Free MoS 2 /FeOOH@CC Heterostructure as Photoelectrode for High-Performance Photo-Rechargeable Zinc-Ion Batteries
abstract: Photorechargeable zinc-ion batteries (PRZIBs) have garnered considerable research interest as a promising alternative to conventional solar charging technologies due to their intrinsic safety, environmental compatibility, and cost-effectiveness. However, the development...

title: Engineered Co 3 O 4 nanoboxes boost luminol chemiluminescence for selective Mn 2+ sensing
authors: 'Tsegu Lijalem', 'Tadele Eticha', 'Ala'a Mhmoued Abdllh Alboull', 'Muhammad Rehan Hasan Shah Gilani', 'Fathimath Abbas', 'Jiyang Liu', 'Baohua Lou', 'Guobao Xu'
abstract: Spinel oxide Co 3 O 4 nanoboxes boosted luminol/H 2 O 2 chemiluminescence by a factor of 670 and Mn 2+ significantly quenched chemiluminescence, enabling highly sensitive Mn 2+ detection.

title: Chromophore-assisted plasmonic catalysis: Au–BODIPY–Pd nanocomposite for efficient Suzuki coupling in water
abstract: A plasmonic nanocomposite composed of gold and palladium nanoparticles, in conjunction with a BODIPY chromophore, was synthesized and explored as an efficient photocatalyst for Suzuki coupling reactions.

title: Controlled synthesis of copper oxide nanoparticles (CuONPs) by the precipitation method and proposition of their growth mechanism
abstract: This work focuses on monitoring copper oxide-based nanoparticles synthesized via a surfactant-free precipitation method by varying experimental conditions and proposing their growth mechanism.

title: Chirality functionalization coordinates with an external magnetic field for enhancing the oxygen evolution reaction of water electrolysis
abstract: The universal non-additive synergy between chiral endogenous and magnetic exogenous spin regulation in three chiral-functionalized CoFeO x systems.

title: Targeting obesity and adipokine signaling in the breast cancer microenvironment for enhanced tumor therapy
abstract: This review highlights how adipokines from breast tumor adipocytes drive cancer progression, therapy resistance, and immune evasion, while exploring nanoparticle strategies to disrupt these pathways and improve outcomes. Created with BioRender.com

title: Quantum simulation of carrier dynamics in nanographene-fused carbaporphyrin tweezers@C60 heterojunctions: role of dihedral-angle engineering
abstract: Extended exciton lifetime is achieved in nanographene-fused carbaporphyrin tweezers@C60 hybrids altering small structural parameter; i.e. dihedral angle between tweezers, without hampering the ultrafast charge transfer from nanographene to C60.

title: Bimetallic NiMnO 3 -embedded laser-induced graphene: a high-performance catalytic filter for electrochemical pathogen inactivation
abstract: Single-step fabrication of bimetallic LIG-NiMnO 3 composite electrodes and filters was achieved. Efficient disinfection of bacteria and enteric viruses was achieved under a low applied voltage.

title: A helical polydiacetylene with enhanced thermochromic reversibility temperature from self-assembly of diacetylene-containing rosettes
abstract: Polydiacetylene (PDA)-based rosette supramolecules with colorimetric functionality exhibit reversible thermochromic properties up to 180 °C over multiple thermal cycles.

title: Amplified spontaneous emission dependence on temperature-induced crystalline phase transition in a solution processed MAPbBr 3 thin film
abstract: Temperature dependence of ASE and PL of an MAPbBr 3 thin film in the 20–300 K range during cooling, highlighting the role of thermally activated non-radiative processes and the orthorhombic–tetragonal and tetragonal–cubic phase transitions.

title: Solvothermal synthesis of spherical CoMnO 3 catalysts for efficient trace ethylene removal under high humidity
abstract: Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide-assisted synthesis yields mesoporous MnCoO x spheres with superior activity and exceptional water resistance, enabled by Mn–Co synergy and abundant oxygen vacancies.

title: Outstanding field emission performance via superior carbon nanotubes dispersion by acoustic resonance mixing and synergistic function-oriented annealing strategy
abstract: Innovatively developed acoustic resonance mixing to disperse CNTs and proposed a function-oriented strategy for CNTs, achieving a breakthrough in boosting field emission performance.

title: A robust in situ adaptive artificial interface for dead-lithium-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: Greatly boosting lithium metal batteries’ cycling stability and high-current performance.

title: Unveiling the underlying physical mechanisms of inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: A multi-physics model reveals that the inverted PSC structure suppresses non-radiative recombination by 14.5% and hysteresis by 47% via optimized carrier extraction and a built-in field, yielding superior defect and band alignment tolerance.

title: Multiscale engineering of triple-phase catalytic architecture: integrating atomic Fe–N x sites, Fe/Fe 3 C nanoclusters and Ni(OH) 2 nanocrystals on S,N-doped carbon for rechargeable Zn–air batteries
abstract: A triple-phase catalytic architecture integrated with atomic Fe–N x sites, Fe/Fe 3 C nanoclusters and Ni(OH) 2 nanocrystals on S,N-doped carbon was multiscale engineered for advanced rechargeable Zn–air batteries.

title: Probing Mie scattering effects of Si-embedded Ge spherical QD arrays using Raman analysis
abstract: We reported experimental observation of Mie scattering effects of Si embedded Ge quantum dot (QD)/SiN micro-disk arrays as a result of a significant enhancement in the electromagenatic near-field intensity.

title: Surface and interface functionalization of graphene and beyond: strategies for targeted applications
abstract: Graphene's exceptional electrical, mechanical, and interfacial properties can be systematically tuned through chemical functionalization, enabling its integration into advanced technological systems.

title: Three-dimensionally ordered macro-/mesoporous bismuth for efficient electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction to formate
abstract: A three-dimensionally ordered macro-/mesoporous (3DM/m) Bi catalyst, prepared via a controllable dual-template wet-chemical approach, exhibits efficient CO 2 electroreduction performance.

title: Hollow porous carbon nitride nanotubes with efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 generation in pure water
abstract: Porous triazine-based carbon nitride nanotubes enable efficient visible-light-driven production of H 2 O 2 from O 2 in pure water.

title: The golden rule to address charge transfer in dibenzenthiols-derivates/gold nanoparticles hybrids
abstract: Luminescent organic molecule–gold nanoparticle hybrids: the golden rule to address charge transfer from Au Np to organic molecule.

title: Improving the high initial coulombic efficiency of manganese oxide anodes: a strategy for in situ construction of an interface of metal–organic framework-derived oxide nanoparticles
abstract: A MOF-derived method for in situ introduced nano-metal oxides on a MnO 2 surface, achieving 87.5% initial coulombic efficiency.

title: Solvent-association regulated electrolyte enables high-rate lithium metal batteries at low-temperature
abstract: A solvent-association strategy is used to construct a weakly coordinating electrolyte, enabling fast Li + desolvation and stable inorganic-rich interphase, granting NCM811||Li cells exceptional high-rate capability and cycling stability at −30 °C.

title: Synthesis and single-particle photophysics of BaZrS 3 chalcogenide perovskite quantum dots
abstract: There has been growing interest in replacing halide perovskite materials because of their toxicity and poor stability, and chalcogenide perovskites have emerged as strong candidates for such substitution.

title: A flexible and biocompatible trilayer coaxial heterogeneous structure microfiber electrode for long-term electrophysiological recordings in freely moving mice
abstract: A trilayer coaxial heterogeneous flexible neuronal electrode was reported, enabling precise and long-term electrophysiological signal recording and exhibiting great potential for neuroscience research, neural prosthetics, and diagnostic technologies.

title: Effect of 2D MXene-to-nanoparticle ratio on the electronic structure of Ti 3 C 2 MXene–CsPbBr 3 nanocomposites
abstract: Controlling the Ti 3 C 2 MXene-to-CsPbBr 3 ratio in the nanocomposite tailors interfacial electronic structure and electrostatics, where optimal dispersion forms Schottky junctions for efficient charge separation, while aggregation induces defect states, bandgap narrowing, and localized potentials.

title: Solution-assisted layer peeling for stable and high-efficiency Zn:MAPbBr x Cl 3− x @PbBr(OH) cyan phosphors
abstract: Solution peeling of perovskite single crystals for the fabrication of stable and highly efficient phosphors.

title: High-throughput screening of two-dimensional ferromagnetic materials with high Curie temperatures
abstract: Via high-throughput screening, this study identified 34 two-dimensional ferromagnets with T c > 77 K. Beyond their intrinsic functions, the construction of composite systems with altermagnetism and sliding ferroelectricity is further demonstrated.

title: Interface carrier transport in van der Waals heterostructures: roles of bubbles, annealing, and electric field screening
abstract: Interface bubbles block carrier transport in van der Waals (vdW) heterostructures, while annealing non-monotonically modulates rectification. Coupled with field screening simulations, this work guides reliable two-dimensional (2D) device integration.

title: Factors governing tumor penetration of nanomedicines: intrinsic and extrinsic determinants
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive understanding of the multifactorial determinants influencing tumor penetration of nanoparticles, highlighting how a systems-level understanding of these interdependent factors can accelerate rational design.

title: Symmetry-driven screening of multifunctional 2D monolayers with valleytronic and piezoelectric properties: a high-throughput study
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) materials that combine valley-dependent electronic behaviour with piezoelectric functionality present exciting prospects for multifunctional nanoelectronics and energy-harvesting devices.

title: Scalable synthesis of spatially confined Ge quantum dots with tunable quantum confinement
abstract: A scalable synthesis strategy for Ge QDs is established through MD simulations, analytical modelling, and experiments. Spatially confined, nanometer-scale Ge QDs are formed within an oxide, enabling room-temperature quantum confinement.

title: Engineering a CeO 2 layer on gold nanopyramids for enhanced near-infrared photothermal conversion
abstract: Precise engineering of a CeO 2 layer (3–7 nm) on anisotropic Au nanobipyramids modulated the dielectric environment, achieving 56.2% photothermal efficiency at 1064 nm, and enabling effective tumor therapy.

title: A near-ambient-pressure A7 phase achieved by high-pressure quenching of few-layered violet phosphorus and black phosphorus
