abstract: The variety of mesopores arising during dealumination in Y zeolites is characterized using 3D TEM. The formation of the mesopore architecture begins with isolated pores in defective regions and expands along preferential directions of propagation.

title: Morphology–phase coevolution driven by oxygen chemical potential in Fe 3 O 4 /α-Fe 2 O 3 nanosheets
abstract: By simply adjusting quartz-tube diameters to modulate the oxygen flux, we demonstrate morphology–phase coevolution in 2D iron oxides. This enables a controlled transition from triangular Fe 3 O 4 to hexagonal α-Fe 2 O 3 with distinct magnetic properties.

title: Amine- and hydroxyl-functionalization of iron nanoparticles for tailoring the properties of Fmoc-FF-based magnetic hydrogels: interfacial design toward biocompatible materials
abstract: Magnetic iron nanoparticles functionalized with hydroxyl and amine groups were incorporated into short peptide-based (Fmoc-FF) supramolecular hydrogels, producing biocompatible systems with high potential for advanced biomedical applications.

title: Cellulose nanocrystal gels as radical reservoirs
abstract: Cellulose nanocrystal gels containing deep eutectic solvents and paraquat function as stable radical reservoirs. The materials undergo colour changes upon UV-irradiation, and may be used to create patterned photonic materials.

title: Unveiling the critical role of strain-induced local structure changes in Co–N 4 single-atom catalysts for enhanced oxygen reduction and evolution reactions
abstract: Strain engineered Co–N 4 single atom catalysts adjust Co–N bond lengths and the d orbital center, boosting oxygen redox activity and delivering ultralow bifunctional overpotentials.

title: Highly efficient charge inversion in dense periodic nanoporous framework membranes
abstract: Monovalent cations induce charge inversion in the TpPa-SO 3 H membrane, enabling reversible cation/anion selectivity via synergistic pore–pore interactions at ultrahigh pore density and saturated ion adsorption in nanoconfined channels.

title: Advanced construction of interface structures for tribological applications: from interfacial synthesis and structural evolution to lubrication stability
abstract: Nanomaterials have garnered significant attention due to their exceptional tribological properties.

title: Suppressed spectral blue-shift in ZnS-coated CuInS 2 quantum dots for efficient luminescent solar concentrators
abstract: Controllable ZnS shell coating on CuInS 2 QDs with high PLQY and small spectral overlap for efficient luminescent solar concentrators.

title: Scalable fluorine-free superhydrophobic photothermal coating based on boron carbide and candle soot for anti-icing and photothermal de-icing
abstract: Ice accumulation on outdoor infrastructure such as transmission lines, wind turbine blades, and aircraft wings under freezing conditions poses severe safety risks and operational inefficiencies.

title: Helper lipids accelerate the mass transfer of cationic lipid nanoparticles resulting in an efficient gene delivery
abstract: The low scaling factor confirms that cationic LNPs fuse upon mass transfer. Lag time decreases significantly for LNPs containing DOPE or DSPC + Chol helper lipids, enhancing their gene delivery efficacy compared to conventional DOPC/DSPC systems.

title: Advances in Printable Flexible and Stretchable Thin-Film Electrodes: Materials, Interfaces, Technologies and Bioelectronic Applications
abstract: The mechanical mismatch between rigid clinical electrodes and soft biological tissues remains a primary bottleneck restricting the stability of long-term electrophysiological monitoring. Printable flexible thin-film electrodes offer a compelling solution...

title: Super electron transfer channels enable Prussian-blue-like nanocarbon felts for ultrasensitive glucose biosensing applications
abstract: Prussian-blue-like nanorod felts (PBL-NFs) greatly boost electron transfer, delivering high sensitivity, wide linearity, and stable and selective detection.

title: Bio-modified hydrogels in electrolyte-gated organic field-effect transistors for sensing applications
abstract: Hydrogel-gated Organic Field-Effect Transistors (HYGOFETs), integrated into a lateral flow system, are employed for biosensing applications using an agarose hydrogel functionalized with avidin.

title: Enhancing thermal conductivity and flame resistance of carbon fiber composites using CNT-infused multiphase graphene resins
abstract: Here, we report the formulation of macropore-infused nanocomposite emulsion thermosets (MINETs) optimized to enhance thermal conductivity and flame resistance as a drop-in replacement for the resin phase of carbon fiber composites (CFCs).

title: Metastable cubic Cu 3 SbS 3 : a facile solution-phase access to a kinetic polymorph
abstract: Metastable cubic Cu 3 SbS 3 nanocrystals are stabilised via a low-temperature solution route. They show p-type charge transport and ultralow thermal conductivity from Sb lone pair induced lattice anharmonicity and Cu coordination distortions.

title: Combined resistive switching memory and multi-state operation in terpyridine-based Pd( ii ) and Fe( iii ) complexes for neuromorphic applications
abstract: Molecular electronic devices with tunable resistance hold significant potential as candidates for next-generation non-volatile memory technology and neuromorphic computation.

title: Phase selection behavior of nanosized GaIn liquid alloys governed by mixing enthalpy
abstract: Within the Gibbs free energy framework, a PVD strategy is proposed via regulating the source state and evaporation pathways to achieve controllable preparation of GaIn NPs with tunable structures across a broad compositional range.

title: Advanced catalyst design and mechanistic insights in electrocatalytic upcycling of PET-derived ethylene glycol and coupled electrolysis systems
abstract: This review summarizes electrocatalytic PET upcycling to C1/C2 oxygenates, covering PET depolymerization, EGOR pathways, catalyst design, paired electrolysis, plus outlooks on durability, separation, and system design.

title: Single-molecule glycan discrimination using a graphite nanopore
abstract: Negatively charged graphite nanopores enable label-free, single-molecule discrimination of structurally similar glycans through distinct ionic current fingerprints.

title: From atom to device: an integrated Se cathode with atomic Co sites and dual-carbon confinement for ultrafast Li–Se batteries
abstract: An advanced Se cathode featuring atomically dispersed Co within a dual-carbon host (Co-NC@CNFs) was resported, enabling ultrafast and durable Li–Se batteries with a capacity of 520 mAh g −1 at 50 A g −1 and stable cycling for over 2000 cycles.

title: Synergistically promoting proton-coupled electron transfer of oxygen reduction with dual atomic sites on high-curvature carbon onions for highly efficient Zn–air batteries
abstract: Adjacent Cr atoms promote water dissociation and collaborate with Fe atoms in intermediate conversion during the ORR.

title: Multi-element mixing boosts exfoliation of layered hexaniobate single crystals
abstract: We introduced multiple metal cations to substitute Nb in hexaniobate single crystals. The high-entropy effect led to much more efficient exfoliation compared to that of the pristine hexaniobate without requiring strong acids or bases.

title: Surface dependent organometallic to covalent transition in graphdiyne molecular wires
authors: 'Alice Cartoceti', 'Simona Achilli', 'Paolo D'Agosta', 'Francesco Tumino', 'Shreya Garg', 'Alessio Orbelli Biroli', 'Giovanni Onida', 'Guido Fratesi', 'Valeria Russo', 'Andrea Li Bassi', 'Sabine Maier', 'Carlo S. Casari'
abstract: First in situ Raman tracking of surface orientation dependent organometallic-to-covalent transition in graphdiyne wires on gold.

title: A novel lightweight ceramizable Si–B hybrid phenolic aerogel composite with excellent thermal insulation and ablation resistance
abstract: This study prepared phenolic aerogel composites with antioxidant and ablation-resistant properties through silicon-boron hybrid modification, demonstrating significant application potential in aerospace thermal protection.

title: Nano-engineered PEDOT(MXene)/PVDF(HFP) bilayer membranes for dual-mode flexible sensing and machine learning-guided signal recognition
abstract: A bilayer PEDOT(MXene)–PVDF(HFP) flexible sensor combines high conductivity, ultrahigh stretchability (1200%), and MXene-enhanced dual-mode sensing, enabling intelligent discrimination of pressure and strain with 92.13% accuracy.

title: Breaking the trade-off with ordered nanochannels for highly effective osmotic energy conversion
abstract: To address the trade-off between ion flux and selectivity in the field of high-performance osmotic energy conversion technologies, we propose a “skeleton-guided self-assembly” strategy, achieving a power density of 29.3 W m −2 .

title: A porous van der Waals single-crystal of aromatic dendrimer exhibiting enhanced thermally activated delayed fluorescence
abstract: A porous van der Waals crystal composed of an aromatic dendrimer was newly found to exhibit enhanced TADF property.

title: Enhancing photon upconversion properties in anthracene derivatives through meticulous control of excluded volume around π-systems
abstract: Anthracene derivatives with bulky substituents showed improved efficiency of photon upconversion (UC) both in solution and in the solid state.

title: Ultrasound-driven triphasic contact-electro-catalytic CO 2 reduction to methanol
abstract: A novel catalytic strategy, contact-electrification catalysis, was employed to achieve metal-free CO 2 conversion. Highly selective conversion of CO 2 to methanol was realized with a selectivity of up to 93.6%.

title: Rationally designed freestanding peapod-like carbon-coated V 2 O 3 nanowire film cathodes enable highly stable aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The unique peapod-like nanostructure of P-V 2 O 3 @C suppresses vanadium dissolution through the synergistic effect of the carbon layer as a physical barrier and V–C bonds with anchoring functionality, thereby achieving excellent cycling stability.

title: Epitaxial growth of hexagonal Pd on Co 3 O 4 /NC heterostructures for high-performance ORR electrocatalysis
abstract: Multicomponent hybrid systems with engineered interfaces are promising candidates for enhancing electrocatalytic ORR performance.

title: Electric and magnetic field-induced birefringence of 2D nanocolloidal liquid crystals with large magneto–optical Kerr effect
abstract: In recent years, the field-induced alignment of 2D lyotropic liquid crystals (2D LCs) has attracted increasing attention due to its promising applications in mechanical sensing, biomedicine, and optical devices. However,...

title: Scalable synthesis of nano-structured sulfur-doped petroleum coke with high-rate capability and long cyclability for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Pre-sulfurized and gradient-carbonized porous carbon boosts Na-storage: hierarchical pores aid ion transport; S-doping expands interlayers and capacity via C–S–C bonds; defects and 3D network ensure stability.

title: One-step microwave-assisted synthesis of a MoO 3− x -covalent organic framework (COF)-based nanocomposite for amplified surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy in environmental applications
abstract: This study first reports a novel one-step microwave synthesis of MoO 3− x -TpTTA COF nanocomposite (NC). The NC enabled SERS detection of methylene blue (EF 7.76 × 10 4 ) and demonstrated high reproducibility for Mancozeb detection with a 12.5 ppm LOD.

title: Dehydrogenase-like stacked MoS 2 nanozymes for cancer treatment through disrupting the tricarboxylic acid cycle
abstract: Unique “potato tower”-shaped stacked MoS 2 nanosheets with distinctive dehydrogenase-like activity disrupt the TCA cycle for tumor therapy.

title: Random sequence-guided crosslinking for on-demand injectable HA–DNA hydrogels supporting neural progenitor cells
abstract: Hyaluronic acid (HA) hydrogels crosslinked through DNA hybridization (HA–DNA hydrogels) offer a programmable and cell-compatible platform for regenerative medicine.

title: Efficient Ce–Ti/coal gangue catalysts via microwave synthesis for NH 3 -SCR: enhanced activity and mechanistic insights
abstract: This study successfully synthesized a series of 15Ce– x Ti/CG catalysts with different TiO 2 loading levels by integrating hydrothermal synthesis, impregnation techniques, and subsequent microwave sintering.

title: Transition metal doping enhances pseudocapacitive energy storage in monoclinic molybdenum dioxide
abstract: Herein, we report a strategy for doping molybdenum dioxide (MoO 2 ) with transition metals (Fe 2+ , Co 2+ , Ni 2+ , Cu 2+ , Zn 2+ ). These doped molybdenum dioxide achieves improved electrochemical performance by enhancing its diffusion properties.

title: Recent progress in sustainable electrosynthesis of chlorine-based disinfectants and derivatives: from catalyst design to coupling strategies
abstract: Electrochemical active chlorine synthesis enables sustainable wastewater treatment via on-site, reagent-free generation. This review surveys recent advances in chlorine evolution reaction catalyst design, system coupling, and reactor engineering.

title: Bioinspired multi-compartment mesoporous nanoreactors: modular assembly and functional applications
abstract: Bioinspired multi-compartment mesoporous nanoreactors.

title: Tailoring the phase transition pathway of Ag 2 Te nanowires via surface confinement: an in situ transmission electron microscopy study
abstract: This study introduces a carbon-encapsulation strategy that alters the phase transition pathway of Ag 2 Te nanowires, which enables a novel transition to Ag 5 Te 3 at 180 °C and Te at 200 °C, forming a Ag 2 Te–Te p–n heterojunction with rectifying behavior.

title: Dual pH-responsive pseudopeptide: hydrogelation and self-assembly into single- and multi-walled nanotubes
abstract: A pseudopeptide self-assembles into pH-responsive gelling nanostructures for potential use in drug release. Changes in pH tune nanomorphology and viscoelasticity, driving a transition from nanofibers to single- and multi-walled nanotubes.

title: Artificial synaptic behaviors of a mobile silver-doped vanadium–cerium oxide memristor with embedded silver nanoclusters for neuromorphic computing applications
abstract: A silver-doped vanadium–cerium oxide memristor achieves analog conductance modulation mediated by silver nanoclusters and ion redistribution, enabling enhanced neuromorphic applications.

title: Prediction of the low-temperature properties of electrolyte solvents for lithium-ion batteries via machine learning
abstract: A machine learning framework leveraging the LiElectroDB dataset enables accurate prediction of melting points, boiling points, and dielectric constants for the rational design of low-temperature lithium-ion battery electrolytes.

title: Ultracompact on-chip coiled waveguide-integrated photodetectors enabled by 2D materials with enhanced responsivity
abstract: A coiled Si 3 N 4 waveguide covered by MoS 2 achieves strong optical absorption and enhanced responsivity in a small footprint, offering a simple route to efficient 2D-material photodetectors.

title: Optimizing the performance of wide-bandgap CZTS solar cells: influence of heterojunction structure, buffer thickness and doping concentration
abstract: The optimal heterojunction structure is related to the doping concentration and thickness of the CdS buffer layer.

title: Persistent, broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity of multi-metal surface phase-modified ceria nanozymes
abstract: A multi-metal colloidal nanomaterial was produced, comprised of cerium oxide, silver, and (Ag, Zn) 2 Ce phases. The formulation inactivated multiple virus and bacteria types while remaining active towards mutants selected for resistance to silver ceria.

title: Palladium-based polyelemental nanoparticles for ethanol oxidation reaction
abstract: The strain effect, ligand effect, and multisite synergy of palladium-based polyelemental nanoparticles enhance their activity and durability for ethanol oxidation reaction.

title: Long-term exposure to nanoparticles alters senescence-associated markers and immune responses in human monocyte-derived macrophages
abstract: Prolonged exposure to nanoparticles modulates senescence signatures in human macrophages. AuNPs trigger inflammation, SiO 2 NPs show low impact, and PET NPs alter p16 expression, revealing material-specific effects on immune competence.

title: A TA–APTES coating as a chemical playground: engineering the shift from chemical traits to bactericidal power in superhydrophobic PU sponges
abstract: A multifunctional PU sponge was successfully developed through the application of a TA–APTES coating, making it suitable for environmental remediation and oil–water separation.

title: Field-directed micro-casting of non-spherical nanostructures: overcoming high-temperature spheroidization for bio-inspired design
abstract: Dumbbell-shaped iron oxide structures were fabricated via pulsed laser-coupled variable-frequency electromagnetic rotational fields, exhibiting morphology and dimensions highly consistent with lunar microtektites.

title: Ultrafast exciton–polaron dynamics in moiré superlattices
abstract: A computational framework based on non-Hermitian quantum dynamics reveals how detuning, dissipation, carrier density, and twist angle control exciton–polaron–polariton behavior in moiré TMD superlattices under strong light–matter coupling.

title: CaO 2 nanoparticle-loaded injectable hydrogel with sustained oxygen release and ROS-scavenging functions for accelerating frostbite wound healing at high altitude
abstract: Multifunctional CaO 2 nanoparticle-loaded injectable hydrogel with sustained oxygen release, ROS scavenging, antifreezing and low-temperature adhesion for high-altitude frostbite management.

title: Emissive perovskite quantum wires in robust nanocontainers: CsPbX 3 confined inside boron nitride nanotubes
abstract: Confinement inside boron nitride nanotubes provides a route to create high aspect ratio, narrow diameter, individually stabilized emissive nanowires from lead halide perovskites with structures critically controlled by the nanotube diameter.

title: In situ synthesis of an S-scheme COF-5@CsPbBr 3 heterojunction for efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: An S-scheme COF-5@CsPbBr 3 heterojunction was prepared via an in situ growth method. The formation of an internal electric field facilitates the fast migration of photogenerated charge carriers, enhancing the activity for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Modulating the electronic structure of cobalt sites using zinc to promote the oxygen evolution electrocatalysis
abstract: Zn incorporated into Co layered (oxy)hydroxides modulates the electronic states of Co through Zn–O–Co bridging motifs. This induces an intermediate-spin state of the Co site, facilitating Co–oxo formation and boosting the OER performance.

title: Two-dimensional Mn 2 CF 2 MXene-based magnetic tunnel junctions with giant spin filter tunnel magnetoresistance
abstract: MXene-based vdW magnetic tunnel junctions exhibit high spin-filter tunneling magnetoresistance with electric-field-induced spin-flip transitions, highlighting their potential for next-generation spintronic applications.

title: Synergistic ruthenium single-atom and nanoparticles in nickel as cooperative catalysts for the alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A scalable one-pot synthesis yields Ru single-atom alloys and Ru nanoparticles embedded in Ni, delivering a Pt-like HER performance and an efficient AEMWE operation with only 0.1 mg cm −2 of noble metal.

title: A balanced ternary 2–9 decoder based on a terahertz metasurface and its sensing applications
abstract: With the development of terahertz communication technology, the demand for multi-valued logic functional devices has increased sharply.

title: Nanocarrier-empowered aggregation-induced luminescence and its therapeutic promise in oncology
abstract: This article focused on the key bottlenecks in the in vivo applications of AIEgens and systematically reviewed strategies for combining nanocarriers with them to address issues such as biocompatibility and biosafety.

title: Interface-homogenized engineering of red phosphorus/activated carbon for high-performance lithium-ion battery anodes
abstract: Surface phosphorylation enables homogeneous integration, alleviating the poor interfacial compatibility of red phosphorus/activated carbon composites resulting from physicochemical mismatches.

title: Label-free surface plasmon polariton application for labelled biomolecule detection
abstract: Surface plasmon polaritons combined with fluorescent dye-labelled biomolecules detection show an enhanced resonance quality factor (Purcell), through temporal and spectral characterization of plasmonic structures.

title: Closing the nitrogen loop: sustainable ammonia production from industrial nitrate waste using a stacked electrolyzer
abstract: Nitrate in wastewater poses a significant threat to the aquatic ecosystem and disrupts the nitrogen cycle.

title: Point defect-driven switching of conductivity type in Bi 2 Te 3 films prepared by atomic layer deposition
abstract: A schematic of atomic migration in Bi 2 Te 3 films. Differences in crystallinity lead to changes in point defects during thermal processing.

title: Nanoscale control over single vortex motion in an unconventional superconductor
abstract: Precise control of superconducting vortices is crucial for studying vortex dynamics and vortex braiding.

title: Octadecene-free colloidal synthesis of CsPbI 3 nanocrystals with improved size, shape and phase control
abstract: This study explores octadecane in CsPbI 3 nanocrystal synthesis, achieving lower polydispersity, enhanced prevalence of the orthorhombic phase, and greater stability.

title: Machine learning assisted stability and CO 2 reduction reaction activity prediction of single atom alloys
abstract: Stability and CO 2 reduction reaction activity prediction of single atom alloys by machine learning integrated with DFT.

title: Coupling external conditions and intrinsic structure in non-noble metal electrocatalysts for the HER/OER
abstract: Focusing on alkaline HER/OER, this review outlines degradation in Fe-, Co-, and Mo-based nanoelectrocatalysts and highlights interface engineering, defect tuning, alloying, and self-supported designs to improve activity and durability.

title: Structure free: compositionally-informed machine learning for solid-state electrolytes design
abstract: Next-generation energy storage demands high-performance solid-state electrolytes (SSEs), where machine learning (ML) promises to be a powerful design tool. Typically, ML requires atomic positions to construct essential material descriptors, which...

title: Quasi-honeycomb graphene architectures enabling geometry-adaptive thermal regulation for high-density electronics
abstract: An ultralight and ultrathin quasi-honeycomb graphene cooling surface that effectively suppresses temperature rise during electronic device operation.

title: Surface-percolated GNP coatings on 3D-printed PLA lattices for tunable EMI shielding and flame-retardant multifunctionality
abstract: Tunable EMI shielding and lightweight multifunctional performance enabled by surface-percolated graphene coatings on 3D-printed lattices.

title: A hierarchically engineered FeCoP/Cu 2 S flake-like clustered heterostructure with benchmark-level trifunctional electrocatalytic activity and long-term durability in challenging alkaline systems
abstract: Rational design of trifunctional electrocatalysts that can efficiently drive the HER, OER, and ORR on a single, efficient and robust electrode remains a key challenge for integrated energy conversion systems.

title: Edge functionalization of graphyne nanoribbons for lithium-ion battery electrodes: a computational study
abstract: Density functional theory (DFT) calculations were performed to systematically examine the structure and electronic properties of 56 functionalized graphyne nanoribbons, assessing their potential as electrode materials for lithium-ion batteries.

title: Nitrilotriacetic acid functionalized gold nanopillars enable stochastic detection and deep learning analysis of prolines and hydroxyprolines by surface enhanced Raman spectroscopy
abstract: We developed a stochastic SERS method to detect prolines and hydroxyprolines within 30 minutes by functionalizing gold nanopillars with nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) and nickel ions (Ni 2+ ) to form NTA–Ni structure for reversible binding of Pro/Hyp.

title: In situ programming of CAR-macrophages via a polyplex nanomaterial and STING activation for triple-negative breast cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Polyplex-driven CAR-macrophage engineering, potentiated by STING agonism, offers scalable, cost-effective immunotherapy against solid tumors.

title: Charges on a suspended silicon nitride membrane under a high-energy electron beam
abstract: Quantitative charge analysis reveals stable residual and localized charges on SiN x membranes under a high-energy electron beam, which could influence particle dynamics, mass transport, particle nucleation and growth in gas and liquid phase TEM.

title: Asymmetric cryptography enabled by TaO x -based complementary memristors with tunable pulse dynamics
abstract: This study demonstrates a TaO x -based complementary memristor array that implements asymmetric encryption and identity-based authentication within memory, eliminating secure channels and enhancing security for resource-constrained IoT devices.

title: Heteroatom (N,Co)-doped MXene with tunable doping for enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction and energy storage
abstract: This study demonstrates that N and Co co-doped MXene, with tuned doping levels, alters electrochemical performance, where the synergistic heteroatom incorporation improves capacitance, charge transport, and the HER activity.

title: One-pot solvothermal conversion of polysilazane to fluorescent nanoparticles for anticounterfeiting and Ag + sensing application
abstract: Polysilazane-derived fluorescent nanoparticles have been synthesized using a one-pot solvothermal method, and their properties have been investigated. Plausible application in anti-counterfeiting is explored.

title: Giant electric field dependent hole mobility of CsPbBr 3 nanocrystal films
abstract: CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals exhibit distinct electron and hole transport mechanism.

title: Functional group engineering in metalloporphyrin-based covalent organic frameworks for enhancing sensing performance
abstract: A “functional group engineering” strategy is proposed to modulate donor–acceptor (D–A) interactions in COFs, thereby enhancing their sensing performance.

title: Laser writing of plasmonic catalytic microchannels on UiO-66 layer
abstract: Plasmonic microchannels are patterned by laser-induced in situ Au NPs reduction on MOF-functionalized PET for reducing fabrication steps and costs towards plasmon-active microfluidic chips design.

title: Size-dependent exciton dynamics in TADF nanoparticles for efficient CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: Optimized 133.7 nm TADF nanoparticles control exciton dynamics, enhancing dissociation and charge transport for efficient CO 2 to CO photoreduction.

title: Multifunctional green pigment with UV protection based on capping agent-engineered Cr-doped hydroxyapatite nanoparticles
abstract: Biocompatible Cr-HAp nanoparticle base can act as both a UV shield and a tuneable green pigment for cosmetic BB cream formulations.

title: Enhancing charge separation efficiency in photocatalytic hydrogen evolution via a synergistic strategy based on point/interface dual-defect engineering in Schottky heterojunctions
abstract: Dual-defect engineering boosts photocatalytic H 2 production by enhancing charge separation and suppressing recombination via a stronger built-in field and higher carrier concentration.

title: Progress on perovskite photodetectors for optical communications
abstract: This review summarizes the material properties, device structure, integration design, and the latest optical communication application scenarios of perovskite photodetectors.

title: Hollow-needle-like nanocarbon with inner wall discriminating chirality
abstract: Pore surface and structural properties are key factors in producing functional porous carbon materials; however, the efficient control of both factors has not been fully investigated. In this study, fullerene...

title: Decay-Length-Guided Electrode Spacing for Wide-Linear-Dynamic-Range CsPbBr 3 Nanowire Photodetectors
abstract: High-performance photodetectors (PDs) operating over an ultrawide irradiance range are essential for sensing, imaging, and optical communication. However, achieving a high linear dynamic range (LDR) remains challenging in low-dimensional PDs,...

title: Recent advances in the design of dual-doped RuO 2 for efficient and stable acidic oxygen evolution
abstract: Dual-doped RuO 2 catalysts synergistically enhance acidic oxygen evolution activity and stability via electronic structure modulation, reaction pathway optimization, and defect engineering, offering a promising avenue for PEMWE.

title: Operando TEM study of a working TiO2 supported Au and Au–Cu catalysts during butadiene hydrogenation
abstract: Understanding the structural stability and chemical dynamics of supported metal nanoparticles under working conditions is essential for the rational design of selective hydrogenation catalysts. In this work, we investigate monometallic...

title: Atmospheric pressure plasma synthesis of monophase bismuth nanocrystals
abstract: Nanostructured single-phase metal crystals with single and well-defined crystal structures exhibit unique, predictable, and stable properties that are distinct from those of multiphase crystals.

title: Distribution of oxygen vacancies and their impact on the charge state of Pt on TiO 2
abstract: Pt supported on TiO 2 (101) becomes negatively charged due to oxygen vacancies beneath nanoparticles. Vacancy-driven electron transfer offers design guidelines for tuning metal–support interactions.

title: Precise control of InP quantum dot growth via recyclable indium adducts
abstract: Master of Puppets: By forming adducts with the Indium precursor, triarylphosphines are able to exert control over InP QD formation, allowing targeted choice of QD size and their optoelectronic properties.

title: Large-area freestanding 2D Ni/Co vertical heterostructures with strong interfacial coupling for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Large-area, freestanding ultrathin 2D Ni/Co vertical heterostructures with strong interfacial coupling facilitate improved charge transport and optimized intermediate adsorption, eventually providing efficient OER activity at ultra-low mass loading.

title: High-temperature oxygen-assisted molecular beam epitaxy of BaWO 4 on W(110): growth mechanism and structural characterization
abstract: Growth of barium tungstate nanostructures by MBE, depositing only Ba on W(110) in an oxygen atmosphere. The tungsten atoms are incorporated into the structure through the interaction between the substrate and the oxygen at high temperature.

title: Rational design of gold nanoparticles functionalized with aptamers for improved West Nile virus detection
abstract: This study employs a multiscale approach to investigate the interaction dynamics between the West Nile virus (WNV) envelope (E) protein and gold nanoclusters functionalized with DNA aptamers.

title: A lung-targeted self-assembled supramolecular nanofiber for synergistic therapy of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
abstract: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) is a fatal interstitial lung disease with limited therapeutic options and high mortality rates. Supramolecular nanofibres RPA@BV targeting the lungs achieve combined therapy for IPF by synergistically releasing BIBF and VS-6766 to inhibit fibroblast activation.

title: Engineering tip-to-tip cubic assemblies of octahedral nanoparticles for enhanced generation of near-field electromagnetic hot spots
abstract: This design utilizes tip-based charge concentration and serial near-field coupling to amplify near field focusing, presenting a unique approach to enhanced nanoscale near-field interactions.

title: Recent physicochemical surface modification approaches to enhance triboelectric nanogenerator performance: materials, processing and applications
abstract: This review summarizes physicochemical surface-treatment strategies for enhancing triboelectric nanogenerator performance, triboelectric materials, physical and chemical surface modifications, and their applications.

title: Dual-site adsorption regulation on a bcc/fcc heterophase PdCuIn metallene for efficient urea electrosynthesis
abstract: A PdCuIn metallene catalyst with bcc/fcc hetero-phase structures could balance the adsorption of *CO and *NH 2 intermediates and promote C–N coupling for urea synthesis.

title: Pd single atoms on nitrogen-doped porous carbon nanofibers for efficient photothermal catalytic hydrogenation of phenylacetylene
abstract: Pd 1 /PCNF exhibits excellent performance in the selective hydrogenation of phenylacetylene due to the synergy of structural, electronic, and photothermal effects.

title: Design and synthesis of hydrogenated carbon nanomaterials for perovskite solar cells
abstract: Hydrogenated carbon nanosheets enable tunable PSC interfaces, achieving 18% efficiency and 1000 h stability, offering scalable, durable, and low-cost electrode alternatives.

title: Recent progress in electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction using atomically precise copper nanoclusters
abstract: This review discusses strategies to optimize CO 2 electroreduction by tuning the electronic structure and adsorption behavior of metal nanoclusters via size control, support interactions, and doping.

title: One-dimensional wide-bandgap semiconductor β-Ga 2 O 3 nanorods for high-performance solar-blind ultraviolet photodetectors
abstract: LPCVD-grown β-Ga 2 O 3 nanorods enable a simple route toward solar-blind ultraviolet photodetectors.

title: Heteroatom-based 2D material BeN 4 : exciting properties, synthesis, and application in energy storage and sensing
abstract: BeN 4 , a remarkable new N-based two-dimensional nanomaterial having anisotropic Dirac cones, has recently been synthesized.

title: Highly selective room-temperature ammonia gas sensor based on a sheet-like Ni x C/ZnO heterojunction
abstract: A porous, flake-like Ni x C/ZnO-1 heterojunction sensing material demonstrates a response value of 7.20 to 100 ppm NH 3 under room-temperature conditions. It exhibits a 4.76-fold higher selectivity for ammonia than for other interfering gases.

title: Inverted red quantum dot light-emitting diodes with a dual-layer ZnO electron transport layer
abstract: By spatially decoupling electron injection and transport, a novel dual-layer ZnO ETL enables the inverted red QLEDs with a peak EQE of 20.7% and enhanced operational stability.

title: Ultra-sensitive mercury sensor based on thin film transistor using flavin self-assembly on monochiral carbon nanotubes
abstract: Achieving ultra-sensitive mercury detection requires precise molecular design and seamless integration with electronic sensing systems.

title: W 2 N 3 nanodot/2D C 3 N 4 heterostructures with interfacial covalent bonding toward Pt-free photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The supramolecular assembly strategy constructs strong covalent bonds between W 2 N 3 nanodots and 2D C 3 N 4 , which accelerate charge transfer and optimize the hydrogen adsorption free energy, hence improving Pt-free photocatalytic hydrogen evolution.

title: A new benzotriazole-containing wide bandgap D1–D′A–D2 conjugated polymer for air-processed ternary organic solar cells with efficiency approaching 17%
abstract: The air-processed ternary OSC attained a PCE of 16.96%, which is higher than that of its binary counterparts.

title: A high-performance low-cost triboelectric nanogenerator enabled by biomass-derived carbon in PVDF nanofibers
abstract: A low-cost, high-performance, and durable triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG) was fabricated by incorporating a porous BC filler derived from agricultural waste, corn silk, with excellent dielectric properties into PVDF.

title: Fusing organic and inorganic materials: exploring enhanced optical limiting in a carmine–MoS 2 hybrid
abstract: The nonlinear optical properties of a MoS 2 –carmine hybrid were investigated by employing an open-aperture Z -scan setup.

title: Strong Effect of Nonpolar Solvent Molecular Structure on CdSe Nanoplatelet Stacking
abstract: We report a drastic difference in stacking behavior of oleic acid-stabilized 4-monolayer (4ML) CdSe nanoplatelets (NPLs) in toluene and methylcyclohexane (MCH), two nonpolar solvents that differ in conformational flexibility of...

title: Activity of ice-binding proteins can be markedly enhanced by protein tags
abstract: Ice-binding proteins (IBPs) regulate ice crystal growth, in particular through ice recrystallization inhibition (IRI). IRI activity can be synergistically enhanced when IBPs are fused to protein tags with moderate activity.

title: Nanoparticle therapeutics in FSHD: current research and future perspectives
abstract: Nanotherapeutics has potential to target DUX4 expression in FSHD through genetic and epigenetic modulation, including delivery of gene-editing tools, epigenetic regulators, antisense oligonucleotides, small molecule inhibitors & RNA interference.

title: Nickel nanoparticle anchored 3D carbon nanotube sponge: a free-standing catalyst electrode for efficient water splitting
abstract: A free-standing Ni/CNT electrode was fabricated on 3D CNT sponge. It exhibits outstanding OER/HER activity, 1.55 V for overall water splitting, and high stability, serving as an efficient non-precious electrocatalyst.

title: Correction: Correction: The coexistence of Dirac cones and Fermi arcs in a graphene/WTe 2 heterostructure
abstract: Correction for ‘Correction: The coexistence of Dirac cones and Fermi arcs in a graphene/WTe 2 heterostructure’ by Wojciech Ryś et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 28209, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR90229K.

title: Photoinduced dynamic gelation and deformations based on molecular crystal microrods stimulated by Z -to- E photoisomerization
abstract: Under visible light, APA crystal microrods dynamically transform into amorphous fibers and gels, driven by the Z -to- E photoisomerization. This process can be monitored instantly with Raman spectroscopy using Ag nanoparticles.

title: A highly efficient surface-emitting light source enabled by silicon nitride photonic crystal cavities coupled with perovskite quantum dots
abstract: A Si 3 N 4 photonic crystal cavity coupled with CsPbBr 3 quantum dots achieves a 12-fold photoluminescence enhancement and highly directional surface emission within ±3.6°, offering an efficient nanoscale light source.

title: Surface phosphating activation of PtMo 6 -Ni-BDC nanosheets for an enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A P-PtMo 6 -Ni-BDC composite is prepared by surface activation of a novel POMOF nanosheet. The active species of Ni 2 P and Pt are formed on the surface after phosphating, enabling P-PtMo 6 -Ni-BDC to be an enhanced electrocatalyst for the HER.

title: Thermoelectrochemistry-enabled phase and morphology control of metal phosphide nanocrystals
abstract: Thermoelectrochemistry during electrochemical synthesis and systematic voltage modulation of metal phosphide nanocrystals overcomes kinetic barriers for crystallization with controlled morphologies and tunable dimensions.

title: Detection of microplastics through an optical sensor array using nano-graphene oxide and fluorophore conjugates
abstract: This work introduces a sensor-array based on nano-graphene oxide and fluorophore conjugates, capable of detecting and identifying different microplastic compositions.

title: MXene-based multi-component conductive hydrogel with synergistic crosslinking networks for high-performance wearable sensors
abstract: A highly sensitive MXene@PDA/PF127-DA/Zn 2+ hydrogel is developed, with multicomponent synergy balancing its mechanics and sensing for fast, stable wireless wearable bioelectronics monitoring.

title: Decoding magnetization and magnetic anisotropy in core@shell ferrite nanoparticles: interplay between cation distribution and spin disorder
abstract: The tailoring of magnetic properties in spinel ferrite nanoparticles for theranostic applications relies heavily on the precise control of their chemical composition and crystalline structure.

title: Controlled construction of nanodisk dimer-over-mirror for near-field enhancement and linewidth shrinking
abstract: We fabricated and characterized a novel nanodisk dimer-over-mirror structure combining the merits of plasmonic dimers and NPOM configurations, i.e. , strong in-plane near-field enhancement, narrow linewidth, and strong polarization anisotropy.

title: Activated carbon adsorption enhanced removal of organic contaminants using a catalytic ceramic membrane with peroxymonosulfate activation
abstract: Granular activated carbon (GAC) was respectively loaded into catalytic ceramic membrane channels (MG 1 ) and connected at the rear end of the catalytic ceramic membrane (MG 2 ) to improve the removal of emerging contaminants via PMS activation.

title: Coupling interfacial chemistry and temperature for anisotropy-enhanced sidewall evolution in silicon dioxide wet etching
abstract: Effect of surfactant addition and temperature reduction on SiO 2 sidewall morphology, showing enhanced anisotropy and a decreased sidewall angle.

title: Strong and broadband second-order optical nonlinearity through multiple coupled metallic quantum wells
abstract: Engineering multiple units of epitaxially grown TiN/Al 2 O 3 coupled metallic quantum wells enables a strong (740 pm V −1 ) and broad (200 nm) optical second-order nonlinearity in the near-infrared.

title: Quantitative evaluation of light-to-heat conversion and singlet oxygen generation efficiencies on ligand protected gold nanoclusters upon near-infrared excitation
abstract: Standardized metrics to quantitatively assess photothermal and photodynamic effects on nanoclusters under one- and two-photon NIR excitation.

title: High surface area mixed lanthanum nickelate/ferrates (LaNi 1− x Fe x O 3 ) via modified carbon templating coated on nickel in alkaline OER
abstract: A solid solution series of five LaNi 1− x Fe x O 3 perovskite electrocatalysts with high specific surface areas were synthesized by a facile carbon templating method, and applied in the alkaline OER as ink coatings on metallic Ni substrates.

title: pH-Controlled Electrodeposition of Diameter-Modulated Co Nanowires: Crystal Texture and Magnetic Properties
abstract: Diameter-modulated (DM) and homogeneous-diameter cobalt nanowires (NWs) were electrodeposited into the ordered pores of anodic aluminum oxide templates from sulphate solutions with pH=4.0 and pH=6.5. The role played by these...

title: Retraction: Recent advances in 2D transition metal dichalcogenide-based photodetectors: a review
abstract: Retraction of ‘Recent advances in 2D transition metal dichalcogenide-based photodetectors: a review’ by Anique Ahmed et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 2097–2120, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR04994A.

title: Intercalation of bismuth nanoparticles into Ti 3 C 2 MXene as an anode material for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Bi nanoparticles are uniformly intercalated into Ti 3 C 2 MXene through a one-step reduction, which mitigates Bi volume variation and suppresses particle agglomeration, enabling stable cycling and fast Li + storage in the Bi/MXene anode.

title: Monolithic photonic architecture for volumetric illumination in plasmonic photocatalysis
abstract: Altering the support morphology of a plasmonic catalysts from nanospheres to an optical monolith increases light penetration, shifting from a paradigm of surface illumination to a tunable volumetric illumination with enhanced photoreactivity.

title: Boosting the OER via the metal–support interaction and H-bond network: amorphous RuO x on fluorinated Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites
abstract: The oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is central to water electrolysis for green hydrogen production, where the development of efficient, stable, and cost-effective catalysts remains a key challenge.

title: Multiscale charge-carrier dynamics governed by surface energetics in facet-engineered NaNbO 3 /FeVO 4 photoanodes
abstract: Facet engineering in NaNbO 3 /FeVO 4 heterostructures promotes visible light utilization and prolongs charge carrier lifetime values, leading to enhanced photoelectrochemical water splitting performance.

title: Molecular structure and dielectric anisotropy of ethanol–water mixtures within a heterogeneous graphene/silica interface
abstract: This work reveals how hybrid graphene/silica interfaces modulate the structure and dielectric behavior of confined ethanol–water mixtures. Interfacial asymmetry induces demixing and directional polarization, leading to dielectric anisotropy.

title: The water, of course! Impurity-induced polymorphism in the self-assembly of interfacial trimesic acid monolayers
abstract: The self-assembly of trimesic acid monolayers yields either the chickenwire or the flower polymorph, depending on the water content of the solvent. Deliberate control can be exerted by wetting or drying the solvent.

title: Etching-based plasmonic sensing mechanisms in metallic nanostructures: advances and applications in food safety and environmental monitoring
abstract: This review highlights the recent advances in etching-based plasmonic sensing mechanisms, emphasizing morphology-dependent performance of metallic nanostructures for sensitive food safety and environmental monitoring.

title: Structurally engineered ZnCo 2 O 4 spinel nanoparticles on ZIF-derived hierarchically porous graphitic carbon for high-performance flow capacitive deionization
abstract: Zn/Co-ZIF derived-ZnCo 2 O 4 nanoparticles anchored on porous graphitic carbon enable high-performance flow capacitive deionization (FCDI) with enhanced desalination capacity, stability and energy efficiency.

title: Ultrafast charge dynamics of diketopyrrolopyrrole-based terpolymers for optoelectronic applications: impact of acceptor concentrations and thermal annealing
abstract: Diketopyrrolopyrrole (DPP)-based terpolymers have emerged as promising donor materials for organic solar cells (OSCs), offering tunable optoelectronic properties, broadband absorption, and enhanced morphological stability through random terpolymerization strategies. This study...

title: Binary solvent-mediated modulation of two-dimensional nanoconfined catalytic behaviors
abstract: A novel size-dependent catalytic performance was observed in the confined catalysis of graphene oxide-based membrane reactors in binary solvents.

title: Type-II WO 3 /NiOOH nanoflakes enable efficient and selective low-bias photoelectrochemical saline water-splitting reaction
abstract: Improved charge transportation after co-catalyst decoration on WO 3 leads to enhanced photoelectrochemical activity in the PEC saline water splitting reaction.

title: Thermal conductivity at solid/fluid interfaces: from adsorption to phonon scattering through the rattle effect
abstract: Of relevance to adsorption, separation and catalysis, fluids in nanoporous materials display intriguing phenomena arising from their underlying structural, dynamical, and thermodynamic behavior.

title: Effects of phosphine ligand denticity on catalytic activity and durability of gold nanoparticles in the oxidation of benzyl alcohol
abstract: Monodisperse gold nanoparticles (diameter = 2–3 nm) protected by tetradentate phosphine ligands exhibited higher activity and durability in aerobic alcohol oxidation than mono- and bi-dentate phosphine-protected Au nanoparticles.

title: Novel chemical mechanical polishing assisted by photocatalysis and shear-thickening for a free surface blade of a Ti alloy using ceria nano-abrasives
abstract: A novel photocatalytic shear-thickening chemical mechanical polishing (PSTCMP) method was developed using a custom-made polisher with a slurry containing ceria, corn starch, sodium bicarbonate, and deionized water.

title: Tough, highly conductive and flame-retardant MXene/NR films based on large-sized MXene sheets for efficient electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: A nano-composite film combining large-sized Al-Ti 3 C 2 T x nanosheets with NR exhibits outstanding electromagnetic shielding performance and excellent mechanical properties.

title: Lateral graphene-metallene interfaces at the nanoscale
abstract: The geometric smoothness governs the energetic and mechanical stability of lateral graphene-metallene interfaces. Machine-learning models reliably reproduce DFT stabilities, accelerating metallene interface design.

title: Tunable icephobicity of surface-grown metal–organic frameworks with nanohierarchical texture
abstract: We identify the design limits of nanoconfinement in MOF based icephobic coatings. The findings demonstrate that once the pore size is small enough, chemical functionalisation becomes key to further suppressing ice nucleation and ice adhesion.

title: Pore-intrusion of polymeric binder in supercapacitor electrodes decreases capacitance
authors: 'Julia Im', 'Xinyu Liu', 'Christopher A. O'Keefe', 'Clare P. Grey', 'Alexander C. Forse'
abstract: A combined study using electrochemical characterization, SEM, gas sorption, and solid-state NMR spectroscopy reveals significant binder intrusion into the electrode pores, which impacts supercapacitor electrochemical performance.

title: Precursor effects and formation mechanism of polyol-synthesized thermoelectric Bi 2 Te 3
abstract: Precursor chemistry is demonstrated to control reaction pathways, microstructure, and anisotropic thermoelectric transport in polyol-synthesized Bi 2 Te 3 , highlighting how subtle synthetic choices impact thermoelectric performance.

title: Photoferroelectric coupling and polarization-controlled interfacial band modulation in a van der Waals compound CuInP 2 S 6
abstract: Photoferroionic coupling in CuInP 2 S 6 enables light-controlled modulation of interfacial band bending, polarization switching, and ionic dynamics, providing a nanoscale mechanism for optoelectronic and memory device applications.

title: Dictating orientation diversity of body-centered cubic superlattices in the self-assembly of polymer-grafted nanocubes through heterogeneous grafting designs
abstract: The orientation diversity of BCC superlattices can be regulated through heterogeneous grafting designs in the self-assembly of polymer-grafted nanocubes.

title: Medium matters: modeling the luminescence spectra and emission decay of LaPO 4 :Eu 3+ nanoparticles
abstract: LaPO4:Eu 3+ nanoparticles have been embedded in different media and the reciprocal lifetime and electric dipole: magnetic dipole intensity ratios have been fitted. The results are satisfactory except when embedded as phosphor in glass.

title: Selective detection of membrane-bound amyloid-β oligomers using SERS “hot-spots”: toward early diagnostics for Alzheimer's disease
abstract: Lipid-coated AgNPs capture lipophilic Aβ oligomers, which are selectively detected by Rose Bengal-functionalized AgNPs through SERS originating from AgNP-proximity-induced plasmonic hot spots.

title: Surface plasmon resonance as a breakthrough tool for characterizing the size and shape of graphene quantum dots
abstract: Innovative D -SPR workflow precisely distinguishes size and shape of graphene quantum dots (GQDs), complementing DLS and HR-TEM with advanced modeling.

title: Sliding ferroelectricity and ferroelectricity-valley coupling in Janus RuXY (X, Y = Cl, Br, I) bilayers
abstract: Two-dimensional Janus transition-metal dihalides RuXY (X, Y = Cl, Br, I) constitute a class of van der Waals multiferroic materials with the coexistence of sliding ferroelectricity and interlayer antiferromagnetism.

title: X-ray absorption spectroscopy perspective on electronic structure and catalysis of metal oxide heteronanostructures
abstract: This mini-review elucidates the electronic structure and catalytic mechanisms of heteronanostructured metal oxide catalysts using advanced X-ray spectroscopy. Graphical abstract image: water image by rawpixel.com via Freepik (www. freepik.com).

title: Light-responsive Au/TiO 2 /PDA coating enhances the antibacterial performance of titanium implants
abstract: Photodynamic and photothermal effects can work together to fight bacteria.

title: Challenges and strategies for enhancing surface stability of high-nickel layered oxide cathodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Surface instability drives structural degradation and interfacial side reactions in high-nickel layered cathodes, leading to rapid capacity fading. This review outlines the key challenges and surface engineering strategies to stabilize the cathodes.

title: Targeting lipid nanoparticle mediated co-delivery of edaravone and kaempferol for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis therapy
abstract: A cRGD-modified lipid nanomedicine encapsulating edaravone and kaempferol achieves BBB penetration and neuronal targeting. Nrf2/SLC7A11/GPX4 signaling was activated, inhibiting ferroptosis and neuronal damage, offering a strategy for ALS treatment.

title: Validation of contact mechanics models for atomic force microscopy via finite elements analysis and nanoindentation experiments
abstract: This work validates the application of Hertzian contact mechanics models and corrections on force-indentation curves obtained using spherical indenters on linear elastic samples, using finite elements analysis and AFM nanomechanical measurments.

title: Enhanced Frictional Anisotropy and Wear Resistance via Bioinspired Hybrid Graphene Oxide -Titania Nanopatterned Surfaces
abstract: In this work, a bio-inspired hybrid surface is developed by fabricating a glancing angle deposition (GLAD)-based titania nanopatterned surface, followed by coating with graphene oxide (GO) sheets. The nanotribological properties...

title: Reductive contact and dipolar interface engineering enable stable flexible CsSnI 3 nanowire photodetectors
abstract: Integrated Al reductive contact, 3F-2NA dipolar modification, and PMMA encapsulation enable stable flexible CsSnI 3 nanowire photodetectors.

title: Ni/Co-doped 1T/2H MoS 2 as a robust bifunctional electrocatalyst for hydrogen and oxygen evolution in both acidic and alkaline media
abstract: Bi-metal doped 1T/2H-MoS 2 delivers superior electrochemical water splitting performance under acidic and alkaline conditions.

title: Correction: MicroRNA-mediated silence of onco-lncRNA MALAT1 in different ESCC cells via ligand-functionalized hydroxyl-rich nanovectors
abstract: Correction for ‘MicroRNA-mediated silence of onco-lncRNA MALAT1 in different ESCC cells via ligand-functionalized hydroxyl-rich nanovectors’ by Rui-Quan Li et al. , Nanoscale , 2017, 9 , 2521–2530.

title: Electronic structure of bimetallic CoRu catalysts modulates the early stages of SWCNT nucleation
abstract: Quantum chemical molecular dynamics simulations reveal that carbon nanotube nucleation on bimetallic CoRu nanoparticles is consistent with d-band theory.

title: Optimizing the supercapacitive performance of MoS 2 by multivalent tungsten ion doping
abstract: This study examined the effect of tungsten doping on the structural and electrochemical performance of molybdenum disulfide (MoS 2 ) in symmetric supercapacitor devices.

title: A step forward: nanostructures for hydrogen storage
abstract: Hydrogen storage methods represent one of the main bottlenecks to achieving a global hydrogen-based economy, particularly for light-duty vehicles. Several classes of materials have been investigated to address current demands....

title: Highly ordered Pd/CeO x inverse opals for alkaline hydrogen oxidation
abstract: Highly-ordered Pd/CeO x inverse opals with different pore sizes were fabricated on glassy carbon electrodes. Inverse Opals with small pores (<50 nm) enhanced electrocatalytic alkaline hydrogen oxidation compared to unstructured electrodes.

title: Designing A Carbon-Encapsulated ZnS/SnS2/SnO2 Heterojunction Secondary Battery Anode Displaying Large Capacity and Good Stability
abstract: Developing structurally heterostructured anodes with good electrical/ionic conductivity is significant to achieve highperformance sodium-ion batteries. Here, a carbon-encapsulated hollow ZnS/SnS2/SnO2 heterojunction anode is developed, which exhibits excellent Na-storage performance and...

title: MXene quantum lands: emerging trends and breakthroughs

title: Efficient photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 to acetic acid using a composite catalyst
abstract: The selective acetic acid formation by Ag@NU-1000-SH under white light without the use of sacrificial agents.

title: Visible light photocatalytic ammonia production on single Cu entities attached to nitrogen-deficient functionalized BN sheets
abstract: A schematic mechanism of photocatalytic ammonia production on single Cu entities attached to nitrogen-deficient functionalized BN sheets.

title: Multi-center bonding and structural integrity in M 6 M 8 B 60 (M = Y, La, and Lu) metallo-borospherenes
abstract: Endohedral Eu doping modulated Y 6 Y 8 B 60 , imparting a magnetic moment of 7 μ B .

title: High performance broadband photoelectrochemical photodetectors based on Cu-doped Bi 2 O 2 Se nanosheets
abstract: Copper (Cu) doping modulates the band structure, enhances light absorption, inhibits carrier recombination, and accelerates interfacial charge transfer to optimize the photoelectrochemical (PEC) performance of Bi 2 O 2 Se.

title: Anisotropic in-plane thermal transport in monolayer ReSe 2 and its modulation through layer control and selenium vacancies: experiment vs . theory
abstract: Modulating the anisotropic thermal transport in CVD-grown monolayer ReSe 2 through defects and layer stacking.

title: Enhancing the Li + storage capability of transition metal sulfides by in situ regulation of the phase conversion in operating batteries
abstract: The failure mechanisms of TMSs involve size-controlled and phase-controlled conversions of the secondary products. Al 2 O 3 can modulate the particle size or phase constitution by forming Al 2 O 3 –TM bonding, resulting in enhanced capacity recovery.

title: Growth of centimeter-scale multilayer hexagonal boron nitride films using metal-boride-vapor CVD
abstract: High-quality multilayer h -BN films were prepared using metal boride vapor chemical vapor deposition.

title: 3D-printed carbon nanoparticle monoliths enabling ultrahigh mass loading of NiCoAl layered double hydroxides for asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: This work demonstrates that 3D-printed carbon nanoparticle monoliths enable ultrahigh mass loading (28.28 mg cm −2 ) of Al-modified NiCoAl-LDH while maintaining fast ion transport and stable electrochemical performance.

title: Substrate thermodynamics control growth and spin coupling in flexible cobalt thin films
abstract: We present a thermodynamics-driven strategy using the TIRA model to control Co film growth. This approach links substrate enthalpy to roughness, optimizing crystallinity, the magnetic proximity effect, and flexibility for wearable spintronics.

title: Precision at the nanoscale: an ‘ionic’ view of composition, structure and properties
abstract: Electrospray ionisation coupled with mass spectrometry provides atomic-level insights into the composition, structure, and properties of nanoclusters while also serving as a versatile methodology for material synthesis.

title: Discovery of robust interlocked ferroelectricity in 2D AgCrSe 2 via chemical vapor deposition
abstract: Large-area 2D AgCrSe 2 nanoflakes have been successfully synthesized by CVD method. the unique SHG signals and room-temperature ferroelectricity will facilitate the development of high-performance ferroelectric memristors.

title: Evaluating the efficiency of touch-spun scaffolds in producing dense cell cultures for tissue engineering applications

title: CMOS-compatible Au doping boosts the hole carrier transport in the CVD-grown 2-inch monolayer WSe 2 films for p-type transistors
abstract: Herein, we propose uniform doping of 2-inch WSe 2 via Au deposition and annealing. The 400 °C-annealed sample shows outstanding stability and uniformity, with mobility enhanced 46 times, promising for wafer-scale 2D integrated circuits.

title: Completely degradable bilayer functional-ionic wood heterostructure-derived nanogenerator for eco-friendly power generation
abstract: A bilayer functional ionic wood heterostructure enables efficient triboelectric energy harvesting via strong polarization from oppositely functionalized cellulose nanostructures and rapid ionic charge transport.

title: Enhanced absorption and fluorescence quenching methods for the quantitative analysis of Cr( vi ) ions using avocado seed-derived carbon quantum dots as pseudo-derivatising reagents
abstract: Dual nanomolar spectroscopic detection and quantification of Cr( vi ) ions by S, N carbon dots from Avocado seeds.

title: Pixel-based AFM-IR uncovers nanoscale lipid remodeling in MPNST cells
abstract: By integrating band-based spectroscopy with pixel-level AFM-IR analysis, this work uniquely semi-quantifies lipid abundance and nanoscale reorganization.

title: Elucidating the role of heteroatoms in hypercrosslinked polymer adsorbents for atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Heteroatom-doped hypercrosslinked polymers reveal that backbone polarity increases total water uptake but does not improve low-RH adsorption. Subsequent sulfonation introduces highly hydrophilic sites, enabling efficient atmospheric water harvesting.

title: Carbon-layer-encapsulated MOF-derived CoS 2 for enhanced sodium-ion storage
abstract: The combination of nanostructure engineering and conductive carbon coating enhances the structural stability of cobalt-based sulfides from both internal and external perspectives.

title: From clinic to ecosystem: sustainable zinc oxide nanoparticles as dual-purpose agents for Big Health
abstract: This review introduces a unified framework of sustainable ZnO nanoparticles bridging human health and environmental remediation for the first time.

title: Enhanced DNA capture in silicon nitride nanopores via localized photothermal convection
abstract: Efficient molecular capture remains a major challenge in nanopore sensing due to diffusion-limited transport.

title: Recent advances in chiral nanomaterials for tumor theranostics
abstract: Chiral nanomaterials enable precise tumor theranostics through tumor imaging, biomarker detection, immunotherapy, and catalytic therapy, establishing chirality as a powerful design dimension for precision oncology.

title: Janus-enhanced magnetic modulation under strain engineering and carrier doping in Fe 3 GaTe 2 monolayers
abstract: Janus engineering under strain and electron doping enhances the MAE and Curie temperature of Fe 3 GaTe 2 via strengthened DMI and exchange coupling.

title: Voltage control of magnetism in Ni–Co oxide mesoporous films: impact of porosity on oxygen magneto-ionics performance
abstract: Nanoporosity in Co–Ni oxide films grown by electrodeposition and air-annealing improves oxygen magneto-ionics and cyclability compared to dense counterparts.

abstract: Here we report a compact, single-component nanotheranostic platform—4TIC nanoparticles—formed by molecular self-assembly of an A-D-A-structured near infrared (NIR) photosensitizer (4TIC) and DSPE-PEG-NH₂. Upon single-wavelength (808 nm) laser irradiation, 4TIC NPs...

title: Laser-induced graphene: from precursor chemistry to process control and throughput–resolution–performance trade-offs

title: Carbon dots in laboratory medicine: synthesis, properties, and applications
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) are 0-D fluorescent nanomaterials with good optical and biological properties. This review summarizes their synthesis, properties, and applications in biosensing, bioimaging and diagnosis.

title: A novel method for rapidly functionalizing perovskite oxides
abstract: The nanoparticle density of the exsolution and the electrochemical activity of perovskites can be rapidly tuned by P–V shock.

title: Decoupling CO 2 reduction and hydrogen evolution reactions on CuZn alloys by constructing asymmetric active microenvironments
abstract: Introducing heteroatoms at the step edge of CuZn alloy creates an asymmetric active microenvironment, enabling effective decoupling of CO 2 RR and HER catalytic activities.

title: Polarization-directed growth of single-crystalline silicon nanostructures
abstract: Single-crystalline silicon nanostructures with sub-40 nm widths are fabricated with controlled orientation using 343 nm polarized femtosecond laser excitation.

title: Multimetallic nanostructures: innovations and applications in environmental remediation, biosensing, and medical technologies
abstract: Multimetallic nanostructures enable advanced environmental remediation, sensitive biosensing, and targeted medical therapies through synergistic catalytic and optical properties—paving the way for sustainable technologies.

title: Supported High and Medium Entropy Nano Alloys from MOFs Catalyze Alkene Oxidation Reaction
abstract: High– and medium–entropy alloys (HMEAs) are emerging as promising catalysts due to their different electronics and very good thermal stability. HMEAs have already exhibited remarkable catalytic performance in several electrocatalytic...

title: Direct synthesis and structural regulation of high-entropy oxide nanoparticles via gas-phase non-equilibrium condensation
abstract: High-entropy oxides possess unique structures and physicochemical properties, offering significant application potential across multiple fields.

title: Flexible metamaterials with near-infrared chirality fabricated by 1D multi-pen tip-based lithography
abstract: A scalable, cost-effective, and vacuum-free fabrication method affords quasi-3D intrinsically chiral silicon fylfot metamaterials with ∼30% near-infrared circular dichroism and mechanically tunable optical chirality via flexible encapsulation.

title: Investigating magnetostatics of Fe-Ni nanosphere assemblies by electron holography and micromagnetic simulations
abstract: The alignment of vortex cores in magnetic nanospheres governs the collective response of nanoparticle assemblies, but the transition from collinear to non-collinear configurations remains unclear. We combine off-axis electron holography...

title: Spacer cations: molecular switches for 2D and 2D/3D perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: This review covers recent advances in spacer cations as molecular switches for perovskite solar cells, focusing on design strategies and how they tune 2D perovskite structures, properties, and performance for efficient, stable devices.

title: Preparation of a novel hydrazine electrochemical sensor using Fe 2 O 3 @ZnO core–shell nanoparticles
abstract: Bio-assisted synthesis of spindle Fe 2 O 3 @ZnO core–shell nanoparticles enables superior hydrazine electrocatalysis via enhanced electron transfer.

title: A functionalization-free plasmonic hole-sphere nanogap SERS platform for reliable on-site analysis and oxide-state classification
abstract: A functionalization-free plasmonic nanogap platform enables reliable on-site SERS based oxidation-state differentiation of arsenic through uniform metal-vacuum-metal cavities with high electromagnetic enhancement and minimal background interference.

title: Thickness-dependent polarization modulation at AlN interlayers in GaN heterostructures revealed by atomic-scale 4D-STEM
abstract: Using 4D STEM we observed that the interfacial electric field of GaN/AlN/AlGaN heterojunctions varies with the thickness of AlN. The phenomenon is related to the strain of the sample, and the corresponding performance of GaN HEMTs can be compared.

title: Electrochemically integrated 2D borophene–graphene architectures for energy and antimicrobial applications
abstract: Electrochemically exfoliated borophene–graphene hybrids (EEBGs), produced via electrochemical methods, offer multifunctionality including antimicrobial effects, improved supercapacitor performance, and efficient HER electrocatalysis.

title: Ultrafast dynamics of relaxation in well-dispersed quantum-confined nanographenes
abstract: Probing the intrinsic dynamics of excited-state relaxation in graphene quantum dots presenting exceptional dispersion stability, precisely controlled number of conjugated carbon rings and photoluminescence quantum yield of almost unity.

title: Recent Advancement of Flexible Electronic Based on Textiles: A Comprehensive Review

title: Interwoven electronic and phononic topologies via electron–phonon coupling and locality-engineered edge states in monolayer ZrBr
abstract: Monolayer ZrBr is a platform for coupled electronic–phononic topology, hosting SOC-induced QSH edge states ( Z 2 = 1) and nontrivial phonon Berry curvature. Kohn-anomaly softening and strong EPC ( λ = 1.26) yield superconducting with T c ≈ 21.7 K.

title: Switching on SERS from SERS-inactive substrates through simple electrodeposition guided by defective molecular layers
abstract: Electrochemical SERS enables Raman enhancement via plasmonic electrodeposition and charge-transfer (CT) resonance, but its mechanisms remain unclear. Here we show that defect-assisted nanostructuring and CT resonance enable EC-SERS on flat metals.

title: Synthesis and modification of UiO-66(Ce) for adsorptive removal of methylene blue and Cu( ii ) ions
abstract: UiO-66(Ce) exhibits a high specific surface area with excellent adsorption properties and ease of functionalization, leading to its broad application in catalytic and adsorption processes.

title: Controllable in situ growth of highly dispersed nano-SiO 2 on steel slag powder for enhancing cement-based materials
abstract: Uniformly dispersed spherical nano-SiO 2 (∼50 nm) was grown in situ on steel slag powder and anchored via Si–O–Si covalent bonds. Maintaining loading ≤10.37 wt% prevented agglomeration, enhancing cement-based materials more effectively.

title: Synthesis and characterization of platinum-decorated iron carbide nanoparticles and their potential for magnetically induced catalysis
abstract: Platinum-decorated iron carbide nanoparticles (Pt@ICNPs) are prepared, characterized, and applied in magnetically induced catalysis for the hydrogenation of various functionalities under mild conditions.

title: Interstitial TM–P pairing in P 3 -coordinated wide-gap quantum dots: spin-selective insulating states and enhanced hyperpolarizability

title: Gadolinium-assisted synthesis of Pt/C electrocatalysts: WAXTS–DSE insights into active area and ORR activity

title: Predicting the optical and thermoelectric properties of the SnC–HfBrCl van der Waals heterostructure

title: Imaging conductive nano-domains induced by Gd intercalation in epitaxial bilayer graphene

title: Ultra-low nickel-doped copper aerogels as highly efficient and stable electrocatalysts for the formic acid oxidation reaction
abstract: Noble-metal-free electrocatalysts offer a promising pathway for high-performance direct formic acid fuel cells due to their low cost, low onset potential, high conductivity, and robust stability.

title: Unravelling the charge state of gold on partially reduced ceria thin films
abstract: Using a combination of surface-sensitive techniques, we systematically track both the growth mode and the evolving charge state of gold from the sub-monolayer regime to multilayer coverages on reduced ceria.

title: Highly efficient and ultrafast gaseous iodide removal by controlling the oxidation degree and size of graphene oxide membranes
abstract: The gas transport pathways and rejection mechanisms in rGO and GO membranes.

title: Circularly polarized luminescent nanoparticles: preparation, performance and applications
abstract: This review comprehensively discusses recent advances in the preparation, critical performance metrics, and emerging applications of CPL-active nanoparticles.

title: Hydrogen-Directed Au-H-Au Chain Networks Redefine the Active Structure of Sub-2 nm Gold Nanoparticles

title: Controlling nanoparticle aggregation via gel point shift in the in situ photochemical synthesis of plasmonic epoxy-based nanocomposites
authors: 'Agustina B. Leonardi', 'Nancy M. Cativa', 'Gustavo F. Arenas', 'Marcelo Ceolín', 'Ignacio E. dell'Erba', 'Walter F. Schroeder'
abstract: Tuning the gel point enables control over primary plasmonic nanoparticle aggregation and the resulting optical response of the nanocomposite.

title: Pyridylamine templated borate-derived Co nanoparticles anchored on N-doped carbon for enhanced oxygen reduction reaction in Al–air batteries
abstract: Pyridylamine templated borate-derived Co nanoparticles anchored on N-doped carbon exhibit excellent oxygen reduction reaction performance and are used for the first time as cathode catalysts in Al-air batteries.

title: Metal-free carbon dots toward bio-green hydrogen evolution via photocatalysis
abstract: A metal-free CD-based photocatalyst, which features interconnected C–N five-membered ring structures with nitrogen atoms acting as active sites, enables controllable, endogenous, and non-destructive H 2 production in biosystems.

title: Ultra-low limits of detection for glucose and uric acid using 3D silver nanostructures decorated with bimetallic (AgAu) nanoparticles as SERS sensors
abstract: This study reports an ultra-sensitive, surface engineered, 3D SERS platform for the label-free detection of glucose and uric acid at picomolar and rhodamine 6G down to femtomolar concentration.

title: Local networks of electrical conductance in hybrid gold nanoparticle–polymer films

title: Development of magneto-optic sensors in the ultra-violet region employing graphene quantum architectures

title: Molecular insight into the efficient & robust design of vesiculated protein nano-cages

abstract: In melamine cation-based perovskitoid single crystals, the distinct Bi 3+ and Pb 2+ synergistically modulate network topology. The Bi-based photodetector achieves an on/off ratio 15 times higher than that of its Pb-based counterpart.

title: Enhancing the electrocatalytic potential of trimetallic CeSmGd-MOFs for oxygen evolution reaction, supercapacitor, and dye degradation applications

title: Magneto-catalytic bio-hybrid bacterial bots efficiently eradicate and remove biofilms in catheters

The graphical abstract was created with BioRender.
