abstract: First-principles calculations show Pd Te substitutional defect forms more readily than V Te vacancy and has enhanced catalytic activity, suggesting Pd Te may be the experimentally identified V Te structure.

title: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy analysis of reference compounds for studying rechargeable Li-, Na-, and K-ion batteries
abstract: We built a unified XPS and HAXPES reference library for Li, Na, and K battery materials, binders, and salts. Optimized preparation and selective Os coating suppress charging and provide reliable fingerprints for SEI and surface chemistry.

title: The manipulation of exchange bias in van der Waal ferromagnetic/antiferromagnetic heterojunction by interfacial coupling
abstract: This work reports a pronounced exchange bias effect in the Fe 3 GaTe 2 /CrPS 4 heterojunction exhibiting an exchange bias field of up to 850 Oe. It also exhibits temperature-induced reversal of the exchange bias sign.

title: Interfacial chemistry modulation of Zn anodes via an EMImI ionic liquid additive for stable aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The interfacial chemistry modulation of Zn anodes via EMImI additive adsorption behavior achieves a stable and dendrite-free Zn anode in aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Controlled HCl etching of Al 2 O 3 nanoparticles toward void-free interfacial contact for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: Controlled HCl etching of Al 2 O 3 nanoparticles eliminates interfacial voids and enhances crystallization, boosting the efficiency and stability of perovskite solar cells.

title: Low-dimensional carbon materials to diamond-like phase transitions under extreme conditions: mechanisms, thermodynamic properties and applications
abstract: From microstructure to extreme-condition phase transitions and applications of LDCMs: (a) LDCMs; (b) spp 2 –spp 3 structures formed under extreme conditions; (c–e) DLC condensation, Medthin™-coated medical implants, and wearable fiber-optic devices.

title: Pneumatically controlled microfluidic synthesis of polymeric nanoparticles for mRNA delivery
abstract: A pneumatic microfluidic platform enables scalable, precise synthesis of polymeric nanoparticles for mRNA delivery, reducing variability and improving durability. PPH nanoparticles show high transfection, low toxicity, efficient endosomal escape.

title: A sequential doping strategy for architecturally controlled BiVO 4 photoanodes with synergistic co-doping enabled by BiOI conversion
abstract: A sequential doping strategy enables controlled Nb/Ba incorporation in BiOI-derived BiVO 4 . Nb enhances bulk transport while Ba enriches the surface, synergistically improving charge separation and surface kinetics for efficient photoelectrochemical water oxidation.

title: Ag/Pd bimetallic sites embedded in g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets synergistically catalyze Suzuki coupling and nitroaromatic reduction reactions
abstract: Transition metal (Pd) mediated C–C coupling reactions are key synthetic approaches among organic reactions and have become an integral part of synthetic endeavors.

title: Boosting photo-induced charge separation by the NaV 2 O 5 /BiOCl nanocomposite for alkaline water splitting and environmental remediation
abstract: The NaV 2 O 5 /BiOCl photocatalyst shows excellent PEC water splitting due to its band positions for photo-reduction and oxidation of water. The nanocomposite also exhibited a superior tetracycline degradation efficiency within a short reaction time.

title: Tetramethylolphosphonium chloride-inspired gold nanoclusters endow a heavy metal ion sensor array by pH-regulated surface ligand–metal chelation
abstract: A fluorescent sensor array for heavy metal ion discrimination based on pH-regulated surface ligand–metal chelation is presented using GSH-templated AuNCs with THPC modification.

title: Study of antimicrobial effects of laser-engineered SERS-active Cu@Cu 2 O nanostructures and their compatibility with human embryonic kidney cells
abstract: Biocompatible Cu@Cu 2 O core–shell nanostructures exhibit excellent antibacterial activity and SERS performance.

title: Emergent inductance in antiferromagnetic systems with spin–orbit coupling
abstract: Emergent inductance is predicted to exist in antiferromagnetic materials with opposite sublattice spin–orbit coupling. The inductance is robust up to sub-THz frequencies with a trade-off between the quality factor and the operating bandwidth.

title: High-valent W-doped CeO 2 regulates Cu–Cd-SSZ-13 for enhancing NH 3 -SCR performance
abstract: A catalyst consisting of W-doped CeO 2 supported on Cu–Cd-SSZ-13 is proposed for high-efficiency NH 3 -SCR. W doping optimizes the redox and acidity properties of Cu–Cd-SSZ-13, while suppressing framework dealumination under hydrothermal aging.

title: Correction: Design, synthesis, and fluorescence property tuning of methyl p -ethynylbenzoate-based [10]cycloparaphenylenes
abstract: Correction for ‘Design, synthesis, and fluorescence property tuning of methyl p -ethynylbenzoate-based [10]cycloparaphenylenes’ by Wanchun Duan et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR02115D.

title: Trace high-entropy doping unlocks a high-energy, high-rate and stable Mn-based NASICON cathode for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A trace high-entropy doping strategy is employed to solve the capacity fading and Na + diffusion barrier issues of Na 3 MnTi(PO 4 ) 3 .

title: Thermal conductivity of graphene-reinforced energetic materials: mechanisms and optimization strategies
abstract: The factors influencing the thermal conductivity of polymer composites with graphene-based fillers.

title: Selective binding of sulphated glycosaminoglycans induces self-assembly of naphthalene diimide into fluorescent nanofibers
abstract: We report the supramolecular polymerization of NDIs upon selective interaction with sulphated glycosaminoglycans, leading to the formation of fluorescent nanofibers.

title: Interfacial mechanisms in the freezing of polymer solutions
abstract: Freezing of polymer solutions plays a central role in processes such as cryopreservation, porous material synthesis, and environmental remediation.

title: Anchoring ruthenium nanoclusters using an electron-donating fullerene carbon matrix for high-performance hydrogen evolution
abstract: An efficient electrocatalyst is fabricated by anchoring Ru nanoclusters on defect-rich fullerene carbon framework, which exhibits outstanding alkaline HER performance due to electronic metal-support interaction effect.

title: Enhanced luminescence efficiency and stability in deep-blue perovskite quantum dots through synergistic Zn 2+ /Ni 2+ co-doping
abstract: The optical performance of (Rb 0.5 Cs 0.5 )Pb(Br 0.6 /Cl 0.4 ) 3 perovskite quantum dots is enhanced by Ni 2+ /Zn 2+ co-doping, achieving a 61% PLQY at 463 nm through synergistic structural and electronic effects.

title: Strain control of the electronic structure in WS 2 homobilayers with 0° and 60° stacking angles
abstract: 2D TMDs combine attractive semiconductor properties with exceptionally strong light–matter interaction. In a bilayer, the excitonic resonances shift upon the application of uniaxial in-plane strain by a stacking-dependent amount.

title: Dual modulation of lysosomal integrity via alkalization and lipid peroxidation: a promising strategy for tumor inhibition
abstract: The CuFe 2 S 3 @CaCO 3 nanocomposite induces lysosomal alkalinization via pH-responsive CaCO 3 degradation, while exposed CuFe 2 S 3 generates ROS. These jointly trigger lysosomal membrane permeabilization, disrupt autophagy and promote cancer cell apoptosis.

title: Ultrafast anisotropic exciton transport in phosphorene
abstract: We reveal that long-range exchange interactions drive ultrafast and highly anisotropic exciton transport in phosphorene, distinguishing it from other 2D materials.

title: Enhanced electrochemical performance of ammonium phosphomolybdate for symmetric and asymmetric supercapacitors in an H 2 SO 4 /KI redox additive electrolyte
abstract: Fabricated asymmetric CA-APOMO//MWCNT device setup for real-time application.

title: Construction of Fe regulated NiMoN nanorods as an efficient electrocatalyst for overall water splitting and urea electrolysis
abstract: Rational design of low-cost, highly-efficient electrocatalysts for the hydrogen and oxygen evolution reactions (HER/OER) is critical to enable sustainable hydrogen production via water electrolysis.

title: Spatially separated bipolar transport and surface electron accumulation in tungsten diselenide nanostructures
abstract: A heavily n-doped surface with a naturally occurring high electron density was confirmed in WSe 2 , while the bulk interior exhibits slight p-type doping.

title: Additive manufacturing of glass with nanoscale features: recent progress and prospects
abstract: This review summarizes recent progress in light-based additive manufacturing of glass with nanoscale features, illustrating how processing, materials design, and optical control jointly support integrated micro- and nanophotonic devices.

title: A FRET-based toolkit for quantifying lipid incorporation into nanoparticles
abstract: We developed a modular FRET-based toolkit that enables quantitative, minimally perturbative analysis of lipid incorporation and distribution in liposomes and lipid nanoparticles, guided by a Poisson-based surface-density model.

title: Unique nano-granular ZnCo 2 O 4 microplate-decorated Pt/C as an efficient electrocatalyst for methanol electrooxidation
abstract: Nano-granular porous ZnCo 2 O 4 microplates uniformly disperse Pt, enhancing ECSA and limiting agglomeration. The Pt/ZnCo 2 O 4 /C catalyst shows higher methanol electrooxidation activity, lower CO overpotential, and superior durability compared to Pt/C.

title: N-doped carbon dots for deep-blue emission and CD-LED device with 402 nm electroluminescence
abstract: The N-doped CDs were synthesized by a facile solvothermal method with 2-amino-5-bromopyrazine and terephthalic acid as the precursors, achieving a deep-blue emission of 402 nm in the CD-LED, with CIE coordinates of (0.15, 0.08), and an EQE of 2.58%.

title: Disentangling melanin self-assembly via nanoprecipitation: from protoparticles to supraparticles
abstract: Few-nm melanin nanodiscs are controllably aggregated using nanoprecipitation into melanin supraparticles 30–100 nm in diameter.

title: Catechyl lignin enhances self-assembly for Pickering emulsion stabilization and controlled-release application
abstract: Lignin nanoparticles were prepared and optimized to stabilize a Pickering emulsion for the controllable release of bioactive molecules.

title: Analysis of lipid-assisted self-assembly of hydrophobic CuInS 2 /ZnS quantum dots into water-stable nanoclusters that perform intra-cluster energy transfer
abstract: A procedure was developed to assemble hydrophobic quantum dots and lipids, producing nanoclusters that are stable in aqueous media. The structural change was associated with a fluorescence red-shift and quenching caused by QD-to-QD energy transfer.

title: Organic–inorganic hybrid p–n junction composite for high-performance carbon monoxide sensing and mechanistic understanding using in situ Raman spectroscopy
abstract: Enhanced CO sensing at room temperature is achieved via a WS 2 @PEDOT:PSS p–n heterojunction, with charge transfer and sensing mechanisms elucidated by in situ Raman spectroscopy.

title: Engineering interface-mediated heterojunction in MOF-derived C-In2O3 microrods and CdS nanoclusters for enhanced photocatalytic H2O2 production
abstract: Photocatalysis H2O2 production is recognized a promising route for addressing energy crisis and environmental challenges. However，the photocatalysis efficiency is limited due to the poor charge separation and weak reaction kinetics...

title: Mechanistic insights and design strategies for Ni–TM dual-atom catalysts on MXene for enhanced catalytic performance
abstract: Study on the close-range dual-atom synergistic effect of Ni-TM@Ti 2 CO 2 (TM = Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn and Ni).

title: Bio-based PEDOT: nanocellulose hybrids as efficient hole-transport layers for photoelectrochemical devices
abstract: PEDOT polymerized in the presence of two NCC allomorphs enables the development of sustainable hole-transport materials that can match the performance of PEDOT:PSS, addressing a key challenge for environmentally compatible optoelectronic devices.

title: Recent advances in perovskite-derived microwave absorption materials
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the research progress on perovskite-based materials for microwave absorption applications and presents critical perspectives on this kind of material.

title: Correction: Synergistic regulation of the Zn 2+ solvation environment and interfacial stability using a rhodamine 6G additive in aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Synergistic regulation of the Zn 2+ solvation environment and interfacial stability using a rhodamine 6G additive in aqueous zinc ion batteries’ by Nuo Nian et al. , Nanoscale , 2026, 18 , 2165–2175.

title: Enhancing multiphoton absorption in atomically precise (AuAg) 13 clusters via 2-/4-mercaptopyridine ligand positional isomerism
abstract: Positional isomerism of 2-/4-mercaptopyridine ligands in atomically precise (AuAg) 13 nanoclusters tailors the electronic structure and LMCT pathways, thereby boosting near-infrared-excited multiphoton absorption and nonlinear optical responses.

title: Carbon-encapsulated PtPd alloy electrocatalysts on single-walled carbon nanotubes for durable polymer electrolyte fuel cells
abstract: SWCNT-supported carbon-encapsulated PtPd catalysts were synthesized by solution plasma. PtPd@C/SWCNT showed strong durability, maintaining performance over 5000 cycles and >50% ECSA after 10 000 cycles (1.0–1.5 V pulse), surpassing DOE 2025 targets.

title: Complex magnetic, electrical and magnetoresistance properties of the coexisting Mn–Fe order–disorder phase derived from nanocomposite perovskite oxides
abstract: A schematic view of cation ordering/disordering across the interface of the grain boundary of two different perovskites via controlled thermal treatment. Such ordering is sensitive to the magnetic properties.

title: Single-molecule detection of amino acid phosphorylation using electron tunnelling currents: toward neurodegenerative disease diagnosis
abstract: Electrical single-molecule detection distinguishes all phosphorylated and non-phosphorylated Ser, Thr and Tyr, revealing the conductance mechanism underlying phosphorylation.

title: Recent advances in carbon dots: from multifunctionality to energy storage
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs), owing to their unique core-shell structure, demonstrate promising applications in fields such as luminescence, electrocatalysis, photocatalysis, and biocompatibility, with particularly great potential in energy storage.

title: Dual-tunable terahertz metamaterial perfect absorption device based on optical pumping and temperature control
abstract: This study proposes a dual-tunable terahertz (THz) perfect absorber that modulates its properties using optical pumping and temperature control.
